













Fundamental Beliefs of the
Iglesia ni Cristo
(Church of Christ)

Series 1989




ERANo G. MANALO









Iglesia ni Cristo
Quezon City, Philippines
199 ,8







Introduction
This book contains the lessons that should be used as guide for the preachers of the Church of Christ (Iglesia ni Cristo) in teaching the fundamental trines upheld by this Church. All of these basic doctrines are written in the Bible or Holy Scriptures. which is the sole basis of our faith and service to God. The truths written in the Bible, which should be heard, understood, and believed by all men in order for them to attain eternal salvation, are introduced in the
lessons contained herein.
The teachings of God should be taught by the preachers of the Iglesia ni Cristo with complete clarity and fidelity - moved by pure intentions and filled with power - to people who have expressed their desire to be counted as members of the true Church which our Lord Jesus Christ will save.


Eraiio G. Manalo Quezon City, Philippines
1990








Second Edltlon 1998

Introduction
All the major religions of the world have their own sacred scripture upon which they base their faith. Islam, the religion of the Muslims, uses the Korarr, the Brahmans have their Vedas; the Zoroastrians, Zend-Avesta; the Confucianists, the King; and the Buddhists, the 'lnpitaka. Butover and above all these is the Bible. The Bible teaches not only morals but also, and most important, the will of God, because it is the word of God. The words of God are written in the Bible. Before proving this, we ought first to understand-
Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How did God reveal His words to man?
A. Spoken by God Heb. 1:1-2
--- Q&A END ---





2 Fundamental Beliefs of the Iglesia ni Cristo

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How were the words spoken by God preserved and how were these words handed down to us?
A. He commanded His servants
to commit them to writing Jer. 30:2
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which book contains the words of God?
A. The Holy Scriptures .......................... II Tim. 3:15
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What makes the Holy Scriptures unique and above all the other books used by other religions?
A. The Bible is inspired by II Tim. 3:16 -17 Instruction: Thus, when the teachings written in the Bible are taught to us. we should believe that these are God's words, His will for us lo obey.
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How were the people commissioned by God to write the Bible prevented from committing errors?
A. The writers were guided .................... Rev. 10:4 Conclusion: Thus the Iglesia ni Cristo affirms that the Bible contains no error.
--- Q&A END ---

Proof
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can we be certain that the Bible we now use is itself the true Holy Scriptures? (We should examine whether it has the attribute of the words of God.) What is the characteristic of the words of God?
A. Jn. 17:17
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What does it mean that the word of God is truth and whyis it said to be the truth?
A. Because God's words have fulfillment Is. 46: 11
--- Q&A END ---
Note: The word of God differs from the word of man: the latter is often proven false and goes unfulfilled, while the former has complete fulfillment and therefore it is the truth.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What are some of the words of God written in the Bible that were uttered in the beginning but destined to take place at
--- Q&A END ---
..:301'nt: later Lime and, in fact, have been fulfilled?
!\, In) The lwo world wars ...................... Mt. 24:6-7
l f;:1rt1lne and poverty ... .. .. .. Ml:.

The Bible 3
(b) Knowledge shall Dan. 12:4

(c) Perilous II Tim. 3:1-5
Conclusion: Therefore, we are assured that the Bible we are using now is the true Holy Scriptures because this has the quality of the words of God: What He says will surely happen.


Promise
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the value of believing in the words of God written in
--- Q&A END ---
the Bible?
/\. May have life everlasting ................... Jn. 20:30-31

Instruction
Thus, what is the strict instruction of the Apostles so that we may not fall prey to erroneous faiths?
/\, Oo not go beyond what is I Cor. 4:6
lti.l'ltruction: Reject the teachings contained in the Catholic Catechism. Book of Mormon, and in other such books used by other religions because they are prohibited by God.

t,J, What is God's warning to those who add to or subtract from the words of God written in the Bible?
/\, 'rtH.:y will be punished and will not
l,c Rev. 22:18-19

Conclusion
We should faithfully obey all the teachings of the Bible taught
In the Church of Christ because these are God's words.





The True God
Theme: The true God who should be known and worshipped according to the Bible is the
, Father who created all things.

Objectlvet1
1. Convince the candidate for baptism to have faith in
God, the Creator of all things.
2. Correct his commonly-held but false belief concerning
t:h.e true God.

Summary
The Iglesia ni Cristo believes in the teaching taught by the Bible that the Father Who created all things is the only true God. There are no three persons in one God; thus we should reject the belief in the so-called Trinity. The Bible also prohibits worship of images or idolatry.
Man should not be content with knowing God. He should also enhance his relationship with God and walk in His ways.

Introduction
Almost all religions have as their objective to serve and worship God. But who is that Godwhom man should serve and worship? Who among the "gods" being worshipped today is the true God? We must first know who is the true Godwe ought to worship and serve. This, our Lord Jesus Christ teaches.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. According to Christ, who is the God that should be known by men?
--- Q&A END ---


l;ud

I, And who is the only true Godintroduced by the Apostles?
The Father from whom are all things Cor. 8:6
Cqnclusion: Therefore, whoever teaches that there are other gocl.s asi.de from the Father contradicts Christ and His l).posUes. Thus, the belief in the Trinity- or the belief that in one Godthere are three persons - should be renounced.

Which teaching of the Bible is violated by the doctrine that I.here are three persons in one God?
/\. He is one God-there is no
other God besides Is. 45:21

,f. What is the nature of the true Godwhoshould be worshipped according to Christ?
/\.. Spirit ......................................... ........... Jn. 4:24

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What does it mean God is a spirit?
A. Without flesh and bones
(no material form) ................................. Lk. 24:39
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Therefore, the true God cannot be represented by a picture or graven image because He is a spirit, without flesh and bones.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Can images graven by man be considered the true God?
A. No, in fact images for worship are
forbidden by God Acts 17:29
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Thus, man should not worship graven images. This is prohibited by God.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the correct way of worshipping the true God?
A. In spirit and in Jn. 4:23
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How is worship of God in spirit and in truth done?
A. Worship His name - do His will ............ Mt. 6:9-10
--- Q&A END ---

Whatare some of the things created by God which manifest Fiis power and Wisdom?
/\. The heavens and the firmament Ps. 19:1-4
Example: What can we see in the heavens created by God? The sun, the moon, the billions of stars, and the planets that move across the heavens not in a disorderly, hit-or-miss
manner but harmoniously in their respective orbits With
amazing precision. This is undoubtedly a manifestation of the infinite power of God.

Conclusion: Therefore, to worship God is man's greatest obligation. We should not neglect this. Thus, you should start attending t:1:?-e worship services of the Iglesia ni Cristo. Whenever you attend worship service, tum your attendance card. If you attend the worship service in another locale, ask for a certificate of attendance from the secretary designated to give these certificates.

Q. Who made the earth where we live?
A. It is God who made the earth

--- Q&A END ---

The Church of Christ believes in the teaching ofthe Bible that U1e Father Who created all things is the only true God. The belief in t.he so-called Trinity, the doctrine that teaches that there are three persons in one God has no biblical basis, hence, it should be renounced. The Bible also prohibits worship of images or idolatry. But knowledge of God should not stop here. Man should develop his relationship with God and walk in His ways.

The True Religion
Theme: Not all churches or religions are of God.

ffw True Reltoton 9
: Even if man uses the name of Christ in his service and even if he can perform "miracles", if it is not the will of God that he fulfills, he would not be made worthy of heaven.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Will all kinds of worship dedicated to God be acceptable to Him?
--- Q&A END ---
/\,, Worship based on commandments
of men will be in Mt. 15:9

J. Suppose such services are done with great sacrifice and bodily suffering (like works of penance), will this then make the worship based on the commandments of men.worthy before God?
--- Q&A START ---
A. No, not even if it involves harsh
treatment of the body Col 2:22-23 NW
"These are all destined to perish with use, because they are based on human commands and teachings. Such regulations indeed have an appearance of wi_sdom, with their self-imposed worship, their false humility and their harsh treatment of the body, but they lack any value In restraining sensual indulgence."
[Bible. The Holy Bible, New International Version: Testament Co:p.tainingThe Old Testament and The New Testament. New York: International Bible Society.(c) 1978.]
--- Q&A END ---




Introduction
To serve God is man's sacred duty. It is evil torefuse cognize God, because this is ingratitude to our Creator. But not all fonns of service or worship rendered by man to God are acceptable to Him. Likewise, not all Christian-professing
churches established on earth belong to God. This, theBible will prove to us.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who testifies that not allof those who claim to know God and Christ shall be worthy of the Kingdom of Heaven?
A. Christ Mt. 7:21
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Isn't it enough that man renders service to God in the name of Christ for him to enter the kingdom of heaven?
A. Not even if miracles are Mt. 7:22-23
--- Q&A END ---

8

Proof
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Were there people whose service to God were not accepted?
A. Nadab and Abihu Lev. 10: 1-2
--- Q&A END ---
Note: These men belonged to the nation of God. But when they rendered a worship different from what God had commanded them, they were rejected; they were even punished.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who else served God with pious concern but was rejected and punished?
A. Uzza, even though he was sincerely
concerned ......................................... II Sam. 6:6-7
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why did God punish Uzza instead of being pleased with his sincere concern?
A. Uzza transgressed a Num. 4:15 Conclusion: Therefore, it is not true that all forms of service rendered by man to God will be deemed worthy or acceptable. Though worship is important to God, He wants such worship
--- Q&A END ---




o .F'undmMntal qf tM lgi.ata nt Crl.stx,
to be done In accordance with His will. Therefore, man ahouJd be 1ure that his knowledge, love, and faith in God is correct and true.

Procedure
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How is true knowledge of God proven?
A. By keeping H1s commandments ........ I Jn. 2:3
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. }Jow caJ1 we show our love for God?
A. Th rough obedience ........................... I Jn. 5:3
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What should be included in our faith in God so that such faith will be deemed worthy of bringing us to salvation?
A. Faith should be made perfect
by works Js. 2:14
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How does the Bible consider faith without works?
A. Js. 2:17
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What kind of faith should we possess?
A. Faith made perfect by works ............. Js. 2:22
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which works should be included in faith?
A. The Father's will should be Mt. 7:21 Conclusion: Therefore, we should renounce the belief that salvation can be attained by man through faith alone - even without works. Such a belief is against the teachings of the Bible.
--- Q&A END ---

7'w '1'lw 11
--- Q&A START ---
A. Because lt ls the Church of Christ which has
been purchased with Christ's blood .. Acts 20:28 Lamsa
"Take heed therefore to yourselves and to all the flock over which the Holy Spirit has appointed you overseers,--to feed the church of Christ which he has purchased with his blood."
(Bible. The Holy Bible from Ancient Eastern Manuscripts: Containing the Old and New Testaments Translated from the Peshitta, The Authorized Bible of the Church of the East, by George M. Lamsa. Philadelphia: A.J. Holman,(c) 1957. "Reprinted under Authority of Presidential Decree No. 285 as amended by PD 400.")
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What was given to those purchased by the blood of Christ?
A. The right to serve God ....................... Heb. 9:14
--- Q&A END ---
Note: Ml;lll cannot perform worthy service to God unless he is in the Church of Christ.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How many true Churches of Christ are there?
A. There is only one true Church ........... Eph. 4:4
--- Q&A END ---




Obligation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is God's will for all men?
A. That they be gathered together
in Christ Eph. 1:9-10
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can this will of God be accomplished?
A. We should be members
of the Church of Christ Rom 12:4-5
Col. 1:18 Rom. 16:16
Q. Why is membership in the Church of Christ necessary for a person to be able to perform worthy and acceptable service to God?
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
Therefore, we should renounce the common belief that all churches are of God. There is only one true Church and that is the Church of Christ. It is in this Church that man should perform his services to God so that these services will be made acceptable. Thus, weshould value the Church because it is here wherein man can serve God properly and attain salvation.

Instructions
1. Return to God, leave your false religion, and join the Church of Christ.
2. Continue listening to the teachings of God.
3. Do not be neglectful of your obligations to Him.





The Church of Christ
Theme: Christ commands that whoever desires to be
· saved should join the Church of Christ (Iglesia ni Cristo).

The Church of Christ 18

accepted Christ as their personal Savior. But what they have failed to understand is the manner by which Chiist will save man. They also have not known who the beneficiaiies of such grace are. In this lesson we will clarify these things.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is Chiist's command to those who want to be saved?
A. Enter in Jn. 10:9
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Where can we find those who have entered in Christ?
A. In the flock or the Church of Christ... Jn. 10:16
Acts 20:28 Lamsa
"Take heed therefore to yourselves and to all the flock over which Lhe Holy Spirit has appointed you overseers, to feed the church of Christ which he has purchased with his blood."
[Bible. The Holy Bible from Ancient Eastern Manuscripts: Containing the Old and New Testaments Translated from the Peshitta, The Authorized Bible of the Church of the East, by George M. Lamsa. Philadelphia: A.J. Holman,(c) 1957. "Reprinted under Authority of Presidential Decree No. 285 as amended by PD 400."]
--- Q&A END ---


Note: Therefore, the Savior Himself commands man to enter or join the Church of Christ. Such command should be obeyed by those who want to be saved.

Reason
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why do all people need salvation?
A. Because all have Rom. 5:12
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is one of the evil consequences of man's sins?
A. Man was separated from God s. 59:2
--- Q&A END ---






Introduction
Many people wonder why it is necessary for man to become a member of the Church of Christ to be saved when they believe lhat they are already serving God in various ways. Others say lhat they no longer need the Church because they have already
12

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Besides being separated from God, what will befall the sinners?
A. They are condemned to die Rom. 6:23
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which death is the full payment for sin?
A. The second Rev. 20:14
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Therefore, because all people have sinned, all
people need salvation to avoid punishment in the lake of fire.





,., Fundamental qf nt Cristo

(./. is it necessary for anyone to be a member of the Church
f Christ to be saved? Is it not enough that he just believes
• in Christ?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Tl1e Church is the righteousness of God.. II Car. 5:21 l':J'ote: To save the sinner, "He made Him who knew no sin to be sin for us." Why? "That we might become the righteousness of God in Him."
Q. What is God's righteousness concerning the sinner?
A. A person shall be put to death
for his own sin ....................................... Deut. 24:16
Q. Who did not commit any sin according to the Bible?
A. I Pt. 2:21-22
Q. What did Christ doin order that He could answer for the sins of those whom He would save, without violating God's law or righteousness?
A. Christ made those whom He would save
His body Eph. 5:23
CoL 1:18
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. In the sight of God, how do Christ and His Church stand - related as they are to each other as Head and Body?
A. They stand as one new Eph. 2:15 Conclusion: Therefore, because of the relationship of the Church with Christ, Christ is nowable to answer for the sins of the Church. Being its head, Christ is responsible for the
sins of His body, which is the Church. This is the only way by which man can be saved without violating God's law. Do
not believe those who say that faith in Christ is enough for man to attain salvation.
--- Q&A END ---
Proof
Q, Because of this, is there salvation for those whorefuse to join the Church of Christ?

Th" Church qf Christ 15
--- Q&A START ---
A. Those outside will be judged I Cor. 5:12-13 NN
"What business is it of mine to judge those outside the church? Are yuu not to _judge those inside? God will judge those outside. 'Expel the
from among
The Bible, New International Version containing the Old Testament
,ncl tJ1e New Testament, New York: International Bible Society.(c)1978.)
--- Q&A END ---

O. Will Christ answer for the sins of those who refuse to follow Him by declining His invitation to become members of His
body?
--- Q&A START ---
A. They will pay for their sins ......... .. ..... Jn. 8:24
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What will happen to those separated from Christ or those who did not remain members of the Chur9h of Christ?
A. They will be Jn. 15:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. If those separated from Christ or those who are not members of the Church of Christ perform acts of service to God, would their services be accepted by Him?
A. They have no right to do so even
if they deify Eph. 2:12
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
The Church of Christ will be saved by Christ because He made the Church His body and He Himself is the head- in the sight of God, the Church and Christ stand as onenewman. This is the reason why Christ was able to assume responsibility for the sins of His Church without violating the law of God which states that the sinner himself should die for his own sins.
Therefore, the belief that salvation is attainable by simply believing in Christ, without membership in the Church of Chrisl, is false and, even worse, unscriptural. The Church is necessary not because it is itself the savior but because it is the one which
Christ will save.

Instruction
Therefore, those who are not yet listed as members of the Church should now take steps to be counted among those to be
saved.

The History of the Church Built
by Christ in the First Century
Why most of us have been brought up in the Catholic Church and not in the Church of Christ.

is the hj_st.ory or the Church built by Christ in the first

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is meant by "the flock" as mentioned in this verse?
A. This is the Church of Christ Acts 20:28 Lamsa
"Take heed therefore to yourselves and to all the flock over which l'he Holy Spirit has appointed you overseers, to feed the church of Christ which he has purchased with his blood."

--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What happened to this Church during the administration of the Apostles?
A. It encountered severe persecution
and the members were scattered Acts 8:1
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What did the disciples continue to do after they were scattered because of the intense persecution?
A. They spread the word everywhere
they went Acts 8:4-5
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What usually is the result whenever the words of God are continuously preached?
A. The number of disciples increases Acts 6:7
--- Q&A END ---








Introduction
The Church of Christ believes that the Church established by Christ in Jerusalem in the first century ceased to exist. It had apo tatized. Thus, the Church of Christ in these last days is tl_ie n':storation of the Church founded by Christ. This lesson will give usa brief account of the events that happened to the Church
built by Christ in the first century.

16

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How great was the growth of the Church of Christ which began as a small flock?
A. The_Church reached the Gentiles ........ Rom. 16:4
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. To what Church did the churches of the Gentiles belong?
A. To the Church of Christ Rom. 16:16
--- Q&A END ---

A. Many ................................................ Mt. 24:11 RSV
"And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray."

(c) 1962.)
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the meaning of "being led astray" as mentioned in this verse?
A. This means departing from
the true faith 1Ym. 4: 1
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How were the disciples led astray by false prophets?
A. By means of the doctrines
of the devils I Tim. 4:1, 3 KJV
"Now the Spirit speaketh expressly. that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils; Forbiding to marry, and. commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth."
[Bible. The Holy 13ible, containing the Old and New Testaments, translated out of the original tongues and with the former translations diligently compared and revised, set forth in 1611 and commonly known as the King James Version. New York: American Bible Society, 1970.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who profess the doctrines or teachings of the devil?
A. These teachings are in
the Catholic Church Faith of Our Fathers, 328 (223)
Manual of Chr. Doc., p. 317
p. 328
"Although celibacy is not expressly enforced by our Savior, it is, however, commended so strongly by Himself and His Apostles, both by
--- Q&A END ---

TM Ht.story qf th.e'Church. Butlt by C t in the First Century 19
word and example, that the Church felt it her duty to lay it down asa law.
"The discipline of the Church has been exerted from the beginning
in prohibiting Priests to marry after their ordination."
[James Cardinal Gibbons, The Faith of OurFathers. New York: P. J. Kenedy& Sons.(c) 1917. (Book))

p. 317
"What does the second commandment of the Church order us to do?
"It orders us to fast and to abstain from flesh meat on c rtain days of the year."
[A Seminary Professor, Manual of Christian Doctrine: Comprising Dogma, Moral, and Worship. New York: Lasalle Bureau,(c) 1949. (Book)]

A. The Catholic F.S.I.J., p. 214
"Father S. You have told me that you are attending Mass every Sunday. and I can well understand that youbecome quite puzzled over many things. You see the priest clad in strange vestments. You hear bells. You see the people alternately kneel and stand and sit down. All this confuses the convert for some time. and wonder whether we will ever be able to learn how to assist at the Mass intelligently, much less
participate in it.
"Mr. J. l have observed these things and have been awaiting your
explanation.
"Father S. The explanation are not difficult to understand once you
realize that the priest deals directly with Almighty God and represents Christ. That is why he is clothed as he is. He wears vestments which are known as the amice, alb, cincture, stole, and chasuble. The vestments he wears are the garments of sacriJice."
[MostRev. John Francis Noll, D. D., LL. D., Father Smith Instructs Jackson. Nihil Obstat: Rev.Lawrence Gollner, Imprimatur: Leo A. Pursley, D.D., Huntington. Indiana: Our Sunday Visitor Inc.. (c) 1975. (Book)]

--- Q&A START ---
A. Yes .............. .............. .. .. .. ...... ... .. .. .. .. T.A.T.I. pp. 49, 47
(671, 669)
p. 49
M 'The first law of history,' declared Pope Leo XIII, as we mentioned
previously. 'is to assert nothing false and to have no fear of telling the truth.' ln confonnity with that Wise principle, we frankly acknowledge the responsibility of the popes in the use of torture and in the burning of thousands of heretics at the stake. Their sanctioning of such cruel and brutal measures is unquestionably one of the blackest stains on the record of the I-Joly Office and will remain to the end of time a cause of obloquy and shame upon papacy. Even when it is frankly conceded, as it must be, that their intentions were good and their solicitude was for the Welfare of the victim's soul, let it still be affirmed that the cruel and inhuman methods used are beyond all defense."
--- Q&A END ---

p.47
"CHURCH'S RESPONSIBILI'IY'
"The Church cannot escape responsibility forthe use of torture nor for the burning of victims at the stake. TheChurch in the person of her pontiffs was responsible for the use of torture; this cruel practice was intraduced by lnnocen t rv in 1252... The pontiff tries to defend the use
of torture by classifying heretics with th.ieves and murderers, a mere comparison is his only argument. This law of Innocent-IV was renewed and confirmed by Alexander IV on November 30, 1259, and by Clement IV on November 3, 1265.
"Neither can the Church escape responsibility for sending heretics to be burnt to death at the stake. The mere subterfuge of having the victim turned over to the secular arm pain of excommunication and interdict npon rulers enforcing the death penalty against heretics."
[John A. O'Brien, The TruthAbout theInquisition. NihU Obstat: Thomas E. Dillon,
Imprimatur: John F. Noll, New York: Paulist Press, (c) 1950. (Book))

Proofs that the Apostasy of First Church Took Place
Theme: The teachings of God from which the Catholic Church has turned away.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which images has the Catholic Church exchanged for God?
A. Images of men, birds, beasts,
and creeping things ............... ..... ...... Rom. 1:23, 25
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Does the Catholic Church admit her worship of images?
A. This is admitted by Catholic
authorities Faith of Our
Fathers p. 164-165 (207)
--- Q&A END ---

"It is in this sense, I take it, that scholastic writers have spoken of the same worship being paid to images of Christ as to Christ our Lord Himself: for the act which is called the worship of an image is really the worship of Christ Himself, through and in the presence of the image
and by occasion of it: "
[James Cardinal Gibbons, The Faith of Our Fathers. New York: P.J. Kenedy&
Sons,(c) 1917. (Book)]

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why can't the Catholic Church deny that what she is doing to the images is really an act of worship?
A. Kneeling down is an act of worship ... Ps. 95:6
--- Q&A END ---



--- Q&A START ---
Q. Do Catholics kneel before images?
A. They kneel ........................................
--- Q&A END ---


FaithoJOur Fathers, p.164 (207)







Introduction
Catholic defenders insist that they have not turned away from Christ and God. In fact, they say, "We continue to worship God and Christ." But how did the Catholic Church tum away from God and from Christ? What they have turned away from are the teachings of God and of Christ and this wewill prove in this lesson.

Images
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which teaching of God does the Catholic Church flagrantly disobey?
A. The making of graven images which they
worship and serve ............................. Ex. 20:3-5
--- Q&A END ---

24


"The doctrine of the Catholic Church regarding the use of sacred images is clearly and fully expressed by the General Council of Trent in the following words: Toe images of Christ. and of His Virgin Mother. and of other-Saints. are to be had and retained, especially in churches:
... the honor which is given them is referred to the originals which they represent, so that by the images which we kiss. and before which we uncover our heads or kneel, we adore Christ and venerate His Saints. whose likeness they represent.' ... The Pagans looked upon an idol as a god endowed with intelligence and the other attributes of the Deity."
[James Cardinal Gibbons, The Faith of Our Fathers. New York: P.J.Kmiedy & Sons,(c) 1917. (Book)]
Note: Hence, what the Catholics say differs from what they do. Even if they deny that they worship images, the fact that they kneel before them proves that they indeed worship
images.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What are the evil consequences of such idolatric practice?
A. It has caused many evil things
in this world ..................................... Rom. 1:28-31
--- Q&A END ---

H P\Dldamnal .S."- ff tlw ll!Mfa nt Crfato
g. Doea the Roman Catholic Church admit that many of her member, have Indeed been corrupted by these evil things?
--- Q&A START ---
A. It wu fulfilled In many Catholics Faith of Our
Fathers, p. 19 (192)
Ml am 1ony to confess that corruption of morals is too often found among professing Catholics. We cannot close our eyes to the painful fact that too many of them, far from living up to the teachings of their Church, are sources of melancholy scandal. 'It must be that scandals come, but woe to him by whom the scandal cometh.' I also admit that the sin of Catholics is more heinous in the sight of God than that of their separated brethren, because they abuse more grace."
(James Cardinal Gibbons, The Faith of Our Fathers. New York: P.J. Kenedy & Sons,(c) 1917. (Book))
--- Q&A END ---

WC)nhip
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the principal form of Catholic worship and why is it
--- Q&A END ---
evil?
--- Q&A START ---
A. The Mass Hist. of the Mass,
p. vi (292)
"The Mass is the center of divine worship, of the homage rendered to God by the Church: it is the Church's prayer par excellence, Christ's prayer offered by the Church to God. the perfect praise and thanksg1vtng, the unique source of the divine gifts and graces of redemption. especially for those who participate in it completely by eucharistic communion."
(Francois Amiot, History of the Mass. Translated from the French by Lancelot C. Sheppard. NihUObstat:JohannesM.T. Barton. Imprimatur:E. Morrogh Bernard. New York: Hawthorn Books, Inc., (c) 1958. (Bookl]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. According to Catholic teachings, what is being done by the priests whenever they celebrate Mass?
A. They make an offering out of Christ . Faith of Our Fathers,
p. 254 (219-220)
--- Q&A END ---

"This oblation of the New Law is commonly called Mass. The word Mass is derived by some from the Hebrew term Missach (Deut.xvi),

Proqfs that the Apostasy qf the Ftrst Church Took Place 27
which means a free offering. Others derive it from the word Missa, which the Priest uses when he announces to the congregation that Divine Service is over...
"The sacrifice of the Mass is the consecration of the bread and wine
Into the body and blood of Christ, and the oblation of this body and blood to God, by the ministry of the Priest, for a perpetual memorial of Christ's sacrifice on the cross. The Sacrifice of the Mass Is identical with that of the cross, both having the same victim and High
Jesus Christ."
[James Cardinal Gibbons, The Faith of Our Fathers. New York: P.J. Kenedy& Sons, (c) 1917. (Book)]
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Does the Bible approve of the Catholic act of offering Christ whenever their priests celebrate Mass?
A. This is an open violation of
the command of Heb. 7:27
--- Q&A END ---

Q. Why is it no longer necessary to offer Christ everyday?
A. This was done by Christ
once and for all ............. .... .... ..... ... .... Heb. 10:10-11

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How does the Catholic Church convince her members that the Mass or the so-called Holy Eucharist is in accordance with biblical teaching?
A. They say, "The Holy Eucharist was instituted
by Christ at the Last Supper" ............ My Catholic Faith,
pp. 274-275 (440)
--- Q&A END ---

"What is the Holy Eucharist? - The Holy Eucharist is a sacrament, the supreme gift of Our Saviour Jesus Christ, which really and truly contains His body and blood. soul and divinity, under the appearances of bread and
"When did Christ institute the Holy Eucharist? - Christ instituted
the Holy Eucharist at the Last Supper, the night before He died... "How did Christ institute the Holy Eucharist? - Christ inst.i1ut.ed
the Holy Eucharist in this way: He took bread, blessed and broke it, and giving it to His Apostles, said: Take ye and eat; this is My body,: then He took a cup of wine, blessed it, and giving it to them. said: 'Drink ye all of this; for this is My blood of the new testament which shall be shed for many unto remission of sins'; finally, He gave His Apostles I.he commission: 'do this for a commemoration of me."'
(MostRev. Louis LaRavoire Morrow, D.D., My Catholic Faith. (A Catechism in Pictures). Nihil Obstat: Henricus A. Coffey, S.J., Censor deputatll.S, Imprimatur: Michael J. O'Doherty, Archiepiscopus Manilensis. Copyright, 1936. 1937, 1951: by Louis L.R. Morrow: Manila, Philippines and The Catholic Truth Society,
Manila, Philippines. [Book)]

Q, What is the difference between the Mass and the Holy
Supper?
--- Q&A START ---
A. In the Mass, they partake only
of the bread or the host What They Ask
About The Church
pp. 75-76 (644)
--- Q&A END ---

'There were vartous reasons for the gradual growth of the custom of giving Holy Communion under the form of bread alone: the danger ofspilling when it was given under the form of wine; the unsanitary and repugnant practice of all communicants drinking from a common chalice; the time-consuming process of distributing under both forms, which became quite impractical as the number of communicants increased and crowds filled the great cathedrals; and finally, the difficulty of reserving the Blessed Sacrament for Communion under the form of wine."
[Msgr. J.D. Conway,WhatTheyAskAbou.tTheClrurch.NlhUObstat:Rt. Rev. Msgr.
--- Q&A START ---
A.J. Burke, Censor Librorum Imprimatur: Ralph L. Hayes, Bishop ofDavenport, Iowa, Dec. 23, 1957. Chicago, Illinois: Fi.des Publishers Association, (c) 1958 (Book))

Conclusion: Thus, do not hear mass anymore or receive the so-called Holy Communion.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. ls it true thc!lApostle Peter was the first pope? According to Catholic tradition, when did Apostle Peter die?
A. In the year 67-68 A.D. ....................... C.E. Vol. 11,
750 (105)
"In the Chronicle of Eusebius the thirteenth or fourteenth year of Nero is given as that ofthe death of Peter and Paul (67-68) "
[The Catholic Encyclopedia, An International Work of Reference on the Constitution, Doctrine, Discipline and History of the Catholic Church. Ed. by Charles G. Herbennann. New York: The Encyclopedia Press, Inc., (c) 1913. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. When did the title "pope" become an exclusive title of the Roman Pontiff?
A. In the fourth centucy ........................ C.E. Vol. 12;
p. 270 (107)
"The title pope (papa) was, as has been stated, at one time employed with far more latitude. In the East it has always been used to designate simple priests. In the Western Church, however, it seems from the beginning to have been restricted to bishops (Tertulliano, 'De Pud.', xiii). It was apparently in the fourth century that it began to become a distinctive title of the Roman Pontiff. Pope Siricius (d. 398) seems so to use it (Ep. vi in P. L., XIII, 1164). andEnnodius of Pavia (d. 473) employs it still more clearly in this sense in a letter to Pope Symmachus (P. L., LXIII, 69). Yet as late as the seventh century St. Gall (d. 640) addresses Desiderius of Cahors as papa (P. L., LXXXVII, 265). Gregory VII ilnally prescribed that it should be confined to the successors of Pe1"er."
[The Catholic Encyclopedia, An International Work of Reference on t't1c: Constitution, Doctrine, Discipline and History of the Catholic Church. Ed. by Charles G. I-Ierbermann. New York: The Encyclopedia Press, Inc., (c) 1913. (Book) I
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---

A. They put a question mark when they
listed the name of Apostle C.E. Vol. 12
p. 272 (II-88)
"LIST OF THE POPES.
(1) St. Peter, d. 67 (?)
(2) St. Linus, 67- 79 (?)
(3) St. Anacletus I, 79-90 (?) "
[The Catholic Encyclopedia, An International Work of Reference on the Constitution, Doctrine, Discipline and History of the Catholic Church. Ed. by Charles G. Herbermann. New York: The Encyclopedia Press, Inc.,(c) 1913. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

(DonaldAttwater. editor, A Catholic Dictionary. Nihil Obstat: Georgi us D. Smith, S.T.D., Ph. D., Censor deputat11S. Imprimatur: E. Morrogh Bernard, Vic, Gen. Second Edition, Revised. New York: The MacMillan Company, (c) 1952, 1931, 1949]

"The Confiteor
"l confess to Almighty God, to blessed Mary, ever Virgin, to blessed Michael the Archangel, to blessed John the Baptist, to the holy apostles Peter and Paul, and to all the sain s. that I have sinned exceedingly in thought, word and deed, t11,rough my fault, through my fault, through my most grievous fault.
"Therefore, 1 beseech the blessed Mary, ever Virgin, blessed Michael the Archangel, blessed John the Baptist, the holy apostles Peter and Paul. and all the saints, to pray to the Lord our God for me. "May the almighty God have mercy on me, forgive me my sins, and
bring me to everlasting. Amen.

Instruction: Thus, do not offer prayers for the dead anymore and do not participate in the activities on the so-called All Souls' Day.

Conclusion
We have understood in this lesson that the apostasy of the Church established by Christ in the first century took place when they abandoned the teachings of God preached by Christ and HisApostles. We have proven that it is the Catholic Church which has abandoned these teachings. Their apostasy continues whenever they disobey a commandment of God. In view of this, we should renounce or reject the beliefs and the practices of the Catholic Church including all their superstitions which might have become a part of our daily lives.



[\ LE S S O N N 0. 7



The Origin of other Churches
Theme: The origin of the multitude of churches that are not of Christ.



Introduction
Some people have the wrong notion that all of the established churches today belong to Christ. Others wonder why there is such a proliferation of churches that are in conflict with one another when, in fact, there is only one church established by Christ. People also wonder how this could happen since almost all these churches that claim to be Christian use one and lhe same Bible. The origin of these various churches was taught by Christ in one of His parables.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. To what is the Church that truly belongs to Christ likened, and what is its origin according to one of the parables of Christ?
A. It is likened to the kingdom of heaven.. Mt. 13:24
33
--- Q&A END ---

.,,,

lc

'I'M Orf{/tn qf other 35

LJ, 'l't1 w'l1c,m i,i fl1u lv<.:11. ;)ccorrllo lo
Tv 1}1,, rlur:li: ur Churcl1 orCJ,risL Uc. 12::32
20:28 Lamsa
"Take heed therefore to yourselves and to all the flock over which the Holy Spirit has appointed you overseers, to feed the church of Christ which he has purchased with his blood."
IJJlble.The I-Joly Bible from Ancient Eastern Manuscripts: Containing the Old and New Testaments Translated from the Peshitta, The Authortzed Bible of the
hurch of the East, by George M. Lamsa. Philadelphia: A.J. Holman,(c) 1957. "Reprinted under Authority of Presidential Decree No. 285 as amended by PD
400.")

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who is the sower of the good seed in his field?
A. The Son of Man or Mt. 13:37
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who is the good seed that was sowed by Christ?
A. The children of the Mt. 13:38
Q. How did Christ sow the good seed?
A. Christ built His Church .................... Mt. 16:18
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How many churches did Christ build, according to the Apostles?
A. There is one l;>ody or Church ............. Eph. 4:4
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What name did the Apostles call this church?
A. Church of Rom. 16:16
--- Q&A END ---

Origin
Why, then, are there many churches today? How have they come about?

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Aside from Christ, was there anyone else who built churches? If so, when did this happen?
A. The tares that were sown by the enemy
while men Mt. 13:25
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who is the enemy who sowed the tares when men were asleep?
A. The enemy is the devil ... .................... Mt. 13:39
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What did the Lord mean when He said that the enemy sowed tares "while men were sleeping"?
A. What Christ meant by sleep is death .. Jn. 11:11-14
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who were those people who were asleep or dead after whose passing away The devil sowed tares?
A. The Apostles ............. .... .. ... ...... ... .. .... . Acts 20:29-30
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What did Apostle Paul refer to as his departure?
A. His face would be seen no more Acts 20:25
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How did the brethren react when they realized the full meaning of the Apostle's statement?
A. They wept Acts 20:37-38
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What did Apostle Paul actually mean by the departure he was talking about?
A. His death ........ .. .. ......... .. .. .. ... ... .. ..... ... II Tim. 4:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How could we recognize those who turned away from the first-century Church of Christ, and what is the proof that this is the work of the devil?
A. They turned away from the faith I Tim. 4:1, 3 K,JV
"Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils; Forbiding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth."
[Bible. The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, translated out of the original tongues and with the former translations diligently compared and revised, set forth in 1611 and commonly own astheKing James Version, New York: American Bible Society, 1970.)
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which church teaches and observes these doctrines?
A. The Catholic Church .......................... F.O.F., p. 328 (223)
M. 0. C.D., p. 317
p. 328
"Although celibacy is not expressly enforced by our Savior. it is. however, commended so strongly by Himself and His Apostles, both by word and example, that the Church felt it her duty to lay it down as a law.
"The discipline of the Church has been exerted from the beginning in prohibiting Priests to marry after their ordination."
[James Cardinal Gibbons, The Faith Q(Our Fathers. New York: P. J. Kenedy &
Sons,(c) 1917. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

Rome?
--- Q&A START ---
A. This is the Roman Catholic Cath. Cat. II, p. 146a
"The Church is called Roman Catholic because its chief ruler is the lawful bishop of Rome."
(Catholic Catechism, Part II. Imprimatur: Rufinus J. Cardinal Santos. Manila: Catholic Trade School,(c) 1961. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

Proof
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What other churches branched out from the Catholic Church?
A. Her offspring is the Anglican Church or the
Protestant Episcopal Church ...... Rel. in the U.S., p. 60
"The Protestant Episcopal Church in the United States is oneof the eighteen independent religious bodies that grew out of the Church of England as it expanded during the great age of British discovery and commerce... The principal characteristics of the Protestant Episcopal Church have thus been derived from the Church of England, which in 1534. after a long process of separation, became independent of the Roman Catholic Church."
(Benson Y. Landis, Religion in the United States. New York: Barnes & Noble,(c)
1965. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What about the other Protestant churches? Where did they originate?
A. Almost from the start of the R formation
and the separation from the Roman Catholic Church, Protestantism subdivided into four
major Colliers, Vol. 19. p. 432
"During the Reformation the new form of Christianity called Protestantism subdivided, almost as it appeared. into four major branches... The fragmentation of Protestantism was subsequently extended by doctrinal disputes within established groups and by claims of new revelation that necessitated the founding of new sects."
(Collier's Encyclopedia, (Vol. XIX) U.S.A.: The Crowell-Collier Publishing Company,(c) 1964. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

Note: The four major branches of Protestantism that arose during the Reformation are Lutheranism, Presbyterianism, Anglicanism, and the Anabaptists.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is there salvation in these various churches which are not the Church of Christ?
A. There is no salvation in any other ..... Acts 4:10, 12
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
We should not be surprised at the number of churches today that are not of Christ. You should believe that there is only one Church that belongs to Christ, the Church of Christ or Iglesia ni Cristo. Reject the Catholic Church and its offsprings - the various Protestant churches.


Theme: Salvation Cannot Be Attained By Faith Alone.









Introduction
The belief that it is enough for one to believe in our Lord Jesus Christ in order to be saved continues to grow in popularity. It is said that the Church is not necessa1y for salvation. That is whymany people do not recognize the value of Church membership. Is it true that the Church is not necessary in attaining salvation? Is faith alone sufficient for man to be saved?

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. According to the Apostles, what should a person do in order to be saved?
A. Believe in Acts 16:30-31
39
--- Q&A END ---


.P\aldanwntal qftM lglnla nt Crf.sto
liQ1D: 1bl1 pu1age le misinterpreted to mean that faith alone, to the exclusion of everything else, Is sufficient for the attainment of salvation.

What did the Apostles do to make the keeper of the prison
believe tn Christ?
--- Q&A START ---
A. They preached to him and to the others
and baptized them ............................ Acts 16:32-33
: It is clear from these verses that the preaching of the Gospel by the messenger of God must precede belief.
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why are preaching of the Gospel and baptism necessary conditions for the attainment of salvation?
A. Because this is the commandment
of our Lord Jesus Christ Mk. 16:15-16
--- Q&A END ---
Note: It was from the Lord Jesus Christ Himself that the Apostles learned the requirements for salvation.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Into what are those who believe in the Gospel baptized?
A. Baptized into one body or the
Church of I Car. 12:13
Col. 1:18 Rom. 16:16
--- Q&A END ---

Proof

A. Any man who enters in Hirn
shall be saved ................................... Jn. 10:9

Remain and Endure
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What does Christ command those who are in the Church to do in order for them to be saved?
A. Endure or remain until the end Mt. 24:13
--- Q&A END ---

A. Suffer also for Christ's Philip. 1:29
--- Q&A END ---

Instructions
1. Recognize the importance of the Church for the attainment of salvation.
2. Prove your faith by means of works, that is, by becoming a member of the Church of Christ and obeying the Gospel.
3. Remain in the Church of Christ until the end.



1---- 'LE S S O N N 0. 9



OneOrganized Church
Theme: The true believers in Christ are in one organized Church, the true Church of Christ.

Introduction
"Believers in Christ" is an expression used by Protestants in referring to those who allegedly make up the body of Christ, the Church of Christ. To the Protestants, membership in a particular church or religious organization is not important; because they hold that as long as an individual believes in Christ he is a member of the Church.
There are two ways by which Protestants reject the importance of the Church for salvation. One of these is by saying that it is enough to accept Christ as one's personal Savior for one to be saved. To include anything else for alvation is considered
43

.... Jl\lndanwntQI .._,.4/tlw lflala nf crtato
equivalent to adding another savior. Another way Is by saying that membenhlp In any one particular Church or organization la not necessary for salvation. After all, for them, the body of all professing Christians worldwide ls the Church of Christ.
Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is there truth to the allegation that the true believers in Chrtst can be found in different churches? How does one become a true believer in Christ?
A. No one comes to Christ unless the Father
draws him Jn. 6:44
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How does God draw people to Christ?
A. By calling him to have fellowship with
Christ ............................................... I Cor. 1:9 TEV
"God is to be trusted, the God who called you to have fellowship with his Son Jesus Chiist, our Lord."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in English Version, Catholic Stw;ly Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1972)
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Where are those people whom God called to be in union with His Son?
A. Called in the one body ...................... Col. 3:15 RSV
"And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in the one body. Anfr be thankful."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962)
--- Q&A END ---

a) The body is the Col. 1:18
Note: The Church is one religious body.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can the people who have been called by God into one Church have fellowship with Christ?
A. Christ created the one new man ....... Eph. 2:15
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How do individuals become members of the one body or Church of Christ?
A. Baptized into one body ..................... I c;or. 12:13
--- Q&A END ---
Note: To become a true disciple of Christ one must be baptized into the one body of Christ, the Church of Christ.


1,:,
Many parts, one body

A. Even though there are many parts,
there is only one I Cor. 12:12 TEV
"Chiist is like a single body, which has many parts: it is still one body, even though it is made up of different parts."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1972]

Conclusion
The true believers in Christ are found in only one organization, the body of Christ - the Church of Christ. This one body is composed of those who have done the will of God and are thereby certain of salvation.

Instructions
l. Reject the belief that thetrue believers in Christ are found in various churches.
2. Believe that the true believers in Christ are in one

A. Christ will reject them Mt. 7:21-23

organized Church which is the Church of Christ.

L I<: S S O N N 0. 10 J

The Church of Christ in the Philippines
Theme: It was Christ who established the Church of Christ in the Philippines by virtue of the fulfillment of prophecy.

Introduction
The Church of Christ which emerged in the Philippines in these last days was established by Christ by means of prophecy. This we will study in this lesson.
Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the proof that it was really Christ who established the Church in the Philippines?
A. He prophesied "His' other sheep which
would be made into one flock Jn. 10:16
--- Q&A END ---

Cl,rht in 11,,. 49
--- Q&A START ---
A. They would be made the Church
of Christ ........................................... Acts 20:28 Lamsa
"Take heed therefore to yourselves and to all the flock over which the Holy Spirit has appointed you overseers, to feed the church of C.hrisl which he has purchased with his blood."
[Bible. 11ie Holy Bible from Ancient Eastern Manuscripts: Containing the Old and New Testaments Translated from the Peshitta, The Authorized Bible of the Church of the East, by George M. Lamsa. Philadelphia: A.J. Holman, (c) 1957. "Reprinted under Authority of Presidential Decree No. 285 as amended by PD 400."]
--- Q&A END ---

Q. Whom does Christ refer to as His other sheep?
A. The prophesied sheep are from
afar off place Acts 2:39
--- Q&A END ---

Q. Who were referred to by Apostle Peter as those who were already called?
A. The Jews and the Gentiles who-became
meinbers of the Church of Rom. 9:24
--- Q&A END ---

Q. Where is this afar off place referred to by the Apostles which will be the origin of the other sheep of Christ?

"From the far east will I bring your offspring, and from the far west I will gather you."
[Bible. A New Translation of The Bible Containing the Old and New Testaments by James Moffatt. New York: Harper & Brothers Publishers,(c) 1950.]

The Church qf Chrt.st In the PhUtpplnH 51
• ... till in the far west men have awe of the Eternal, and in the east they see his brilliant deeds; for his vengeance pours out like a pent-up stream, driven by a blast of wind."
(Bible. A New Translation of the Bible Containing the Old and New Testaments by James Moffatt. New York: Harper & Brothers Publishers,(c) 1950.]
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Where in the Far West will the prophecy begin to be fulfilled?
A. In Hawaii and California ............ World Almanac '76, p. 88
"Far West California Nevada Oregon Washington Alaska Hawaii. "
[TheWorldAlmanacandBookoJFacts, 1976. Ed. by George E. Delmy. NewYork: Newspaper Enterprise Association,(c) 1975.)
--- Q&A END ---

Note: This was fulfilled when the Church of Christ went overseas when Brother Eran.o Manalo officiated the first worship service in Hawaii on July 27, 1968. This was followed by another gathering of the brethren in San Francisco, California. These are the official beginnings of the Church of Christ in the Far West.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Was the time of the emergence of the Church of Christ in the Philippines also prophesied?
A. At the ends of the earth .................... Is. 43:5-6
--- Q&A END ---
Note: The "ends of the earth" is distinct from the "end of the earth". What is the difference?

--- Q&A START ---
Q. When is the "end of the earth"?
A. The second coming of Christ ............. Mt. 24:3 KJV

Nol,1;: The prophesied world war is that which broke out on July 27, 1914 which is called First World War. Therefore, the time "ends of the earth" began in July, 1914. This is the prophesied time for the emergence of the Church of Christ in the Philippines. As a fulfillment of this prophecy, the Church of Christ was registered with the Philippine government on July 27, 1914.

[Bible. The New English Bible. Oxford: Oxford University Press,(c) 1961, 1970)

Promise

Conclusion
Thus, in order to be included among those recognized by God as His sons and daughters in the last days, you shouldbecome members of the Church of Christ. You must understand Lhc:11.
God is calling you.
This Church which emerged in the Philippines (in the Far
East) in these last days was established by virtue or the ment of tlie prophecies of God and of Christ. This isthe third group of pI eople in the Church built by Christ. This emerged in these last days after the Church established by Christ in the first century was completely led to apostasy by the false Even the spread of this Church in the Far West is alsoa fulfillment of the prophecy of God written in lhe Bible.





Brother Felix Y. Manalo: God's Messenger
in the Last Days
Th,e,ne: Brother Felix Y. Manalo is God's messenger in the last days.


Introduction
Brother Felix Manalo is the Messenger of God whom He sent to bring about the emergence of the Church of Chrtst in the Philippines. Some may ask, "How did Brother Felix Manalo become a messenger of God?" Are there prophecies in the Bible regarding his commissioning?

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What authority did God give Brother Felix Y. Manalo?
A. Chosen servant with a covenant
to deify Is. 41:9-10 KJV

[Bible. The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, translated out of the original tongues and with the former translations diligently compared and revised, set forth in 1611 and commonly known as the King James Version, New York: American Bible Society, 1970.]

Conclusion
Therefore, this Church, the Iglesia ni Cristo (Church of Christ), belongs to God and to Christ. Brother Felix Y. Manalo is God's messenger, and this we should accept with faith.



L E S S O N N 0. 12



The Promise for Those Who Endure
Persecution
Theme: The reason behind the persecution suffered by members of the Church of Christ and the promise to those who remain faithful and firm in their faith.



Introduction
Fear of persecutions may hinder a person from joining the Church of Christ. There are instances when a man who has just started to listen to the teachings of the Church encounters persecutions immediately and is even prevented from continuing his biblical studies. Such events have caused some to quit from attending Bible Study on Doctrines in spite of their having understood the truth. To prevent such an unfortunate occurrence, it is necessary for us to know what persecution is about and how to overcome it so that it would not destroy our chances of attaining salvation.

59

Ii(/
.Fundamental B I qf tM Iglesia nt Cristo

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What would Christ's disciples face in the world as forewarned by Him?
A. They Will be Jn. 15:20
--- Q&A END ---

/\. Cl1, ist. was hated ahead of us. Jn. 15:18

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Wliy are we persecuted and hated by the world?
--- Q&A END ---
J\. Because Christ has chosen us ............... Jn. 15:19

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the reason why we, the chosen people of Christ, are being persecuted and hated by the world?
A. We are persecuted for His name's sake... Jn. 15:21
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Should we feel bitter when experiencing persecutions because of our membership in the Church of Christ?
A. We may be reviled but we are Mt.5:10-12
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who will be our first persecutors as foretold by Christ?
A. Our own Mt. 10:36
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. When we experience this kind of persecution, what does Christ expect from us?
A. We should love Christ Mt. 10:37
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Should we be ashamed when reproached or discredited for being members of the Church of Christ?
A. Blessed are those who are reproached
because of Him I Pt. 4: 14-16 RSV
"If you are reproached for the name of Christ, you are blessed, because the spirit of glory and of God rests upon you. But let none of you suffer asa murderer, or a thief, or a wrongdoer, ora m'aker; yet if one suffers as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but
under that name let him glorify God."
--- Q&A END ---

[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press
(c) 1962.]

Stand

A. The Apostles rejoiced in their sufferings
for the name of Christ Acts 5:40-42
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What if our enemies threaten us with bodily harm or even death forfollowing the will of God, should webe intimidated?
A. Daniel was not intimidated by threats ... Dan. 6:7-10 Instruction: We should not quit from attending Bible Study on Doctrines amidst the persecutions we are now experiencing so that thechance of salvation offered to us will not be wasted.
--- Q&A END ---

Promise
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is God's promise to those who willingly endure sufferings for following Christ?
A. They will be rewarded with eternal life ... Lk. 18:28-30
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What can those who endure hardships and persecutions for being members of the Church of Christ expect?
A. They will be made worthy
of the kingdom ...................................... II Thess. 1:4-5
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What will the Church of Christ members no longer suffer in God's kingdom?
A. They will never again experience
hunger, thirst, or pain .......................... Rev. 7:13-17
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
Thus, for us to reach our desired salvation, we should endure all kinds of trials and persecutions. Persecutions cannot

, i " ./il111dn111;;;11/u/ "/ l111t: ti111/ Crl:.I
be avoided but If we triumph over them, we shall- be counted worthy In Inheriting the kingdom of heaven. Thus, whatever peraecutlons a member of the Iglesia ni Cristo encounters, he should not falter or withdraw. He should instead pray to God for assistance to overcome persecutions.



LE S S O N N O. 13



Membership in the Church of Christ
Theme: The proper way of becoming a member of the Church of Christ.




Introduction
Whoever joins the Church of Christ with wrong motives will not remain but will be separated from the Church sooner or later. Such people will not benefit from Christ's work of salvation for the Church. Thus, to prevent such an eventuality, it is necessary for a person joining the Church to know the proper way of

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How should one enter the Church of Christ?
A. Enter by Rom 5:2
Jerusalem Bible
" ... since it is by faith and through Jesus that wehave entered this state of grace in which we can boast about looking forward to God's glory."
--- Q&A END ---

[Bible. The Jerusalem Bible, With Abridged Introductions and Notes, London: Darton, Longman and Todd,(c) 1966, 1967, 1968)

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What if one enters the Church without faith?
A. This will not please God .................... Heh. 11:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is God's judgment for those who do not have faith?
A. They are already Jn. 3:18 CEV
"No one who has faith in God"s Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
--- Q&A END ---

[Bible. God's Promise For People of Today, Contemporary English Version, U.S.A.: Thomas Nelson Inc.,(c) 1995)

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What condemnation does God lay in store for the unbelievers?
A. Punishment in the lake of fire Rev. 21:8
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Thus, one should join the Church of Christ for the right reason - not just togive in to the wishes of parents, wife or husband. brother or sister, employer, or for any personal gain or selfish interest.

Explanation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is faith according to the Bible?
A. Having full confidence in the things
we hope Heb. 11:1
Phillips Modem English
"Now faith means that we have full confidence in the thirigs we hope for, it means being certain of things we cannot see."
[Bible. The New Testament in Modern English, translated by J.B. Phillips. Great Britain: Richard Clay {The Chaucer Press) Ltd.,(c) 1960, 1972.)
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why are the faithful confident in their hope?
A. They rely on God's Rom. 4:18-21 NN
"Against all hope, Abraham in hope believed and so became the father of many nations, just as it had been said to him, 'So shall your offspring be.' Without weakening in his faith, he faced the fact that his body was as good as dead ..:. since he was about a hundred years old - and that Sarah's womb was also dead. Yet he did not waver through unbelief regarding the promise of God, but was strengthened in his faith and gave glory to God, belng fully persuaded that God had power to do what he had promised."
[Bible. The Holy Bible, New International Version containing the Old Testament and the New Testament. New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.)
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can the man of faith be recognized?
A. He is strong and firm and cannot be Col. 1:23 RSV
"... provided that you continue in the faith, stable and steadfast,
not shifting from the hope of the gospel which you heard, which has been preached to every creature under heaven, and bf which I, Paul, became a minister."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. NewYork: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.)
--- Q&A END ---

/\frmlvrsl,tr, 111,11111Cl11wr1, qfC/,rl-li

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can one be made firm in his faith?
A. He listens and Mt. 7:24-25
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the evil consequence ifwe do not obey what we have heard?
A. The member who does not
obey will eventually fall Mt. 7:26-27
--- Q&A END ---
Instruction: All the teachings and rules of discipline of the Church should be followed because obedience to all of these is the mark of a true member of the Church.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who are the foremost ones whom we should believe?
A. 1) God and Jn. 14:1-2
2) The Mk. 16!1.5-16
3) God's Jn. 6:29
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
The proper way of becoming a member of the Church of Christ is through faith in God and in Christ. This should be accompanied by the recognition of the importance of the Church and of God's commissioning.
If a man joins the Church of Christ properly he can obey all
the commandments, and this proves that he is a true member of the Church. He is not easily offended; he will not draw back. Instead, he will remain firm and strong in his election.


·· -,-· ....··-----···-----------
L F; S S O N N 0. 14
. ---------------- ----


The Lord Jesus Christ
(Part I)

Theme: The attributes of Christ and His true nature.

Introduction
It is important foreveryone to know the belief of the Church of Christ concerning Christ because Godhas placed Christ in the position of Redeemer and Savior of man. Heis the founder and head of the Church of Christ. Thus, it is of prime importance that anyone who joins the Church of Christ should know the attributes of Christ and howweshould recognize Him. What His attributes are and what His nature is, are the things we shall study in this lesson.
68

'I ,Jc, -,1.'l Cl1r1.s1.
The attributes of Christ
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How do members of the Church of Christ recognize Christ? A Son of God - according to the Father
--- Q&A END ---
Himself Mt. 3:17
Note: Never has any correct translation of the Bible said that Christ is Godthe Son. What can be read in the Bible is the fact that Christ is the Son of God.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Aside from being the Son of God, what else did God give Him as attributes?
A. 1) He was made Lord Acts 2:36
2) He was made Savior. Acts 5:31
3) Mediator ....................................... I Tim. 2:5
4) Placed above all Eph. 1:20-22
--- Q&A END ---
Note: These attributes of Christ which the Bible teaches are recognized and accepted by the Church of Christ. Toe accusation that wearedegrading Christ has no factual basis.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. In view of the attributes given to Christ, what has God commanded every man?
A. God highly exalted Him and commanded
that He be worshipped ...................... Philip. 2:9-11
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What else are the other attributes of Christ which is why the Father gave Him this honor?
A. He is humble and obedient ............... Philip. 2:5, 8
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Thus, even if God has placed all things under Christ, what
--- Q&A END ---
will God do to Him at the end?
--- Q&A START ---
A. He will be subjected to I Cor. 15:27-28
--- Q&A END ---


--- Q&A START ---
Q. What makes Christ greater and above other ordinary men though He Himself is man by nature?
A. All men have sinned ......................... Rom. 5:12
--- Q&A END ---

ff) Jl'undanwntal q/C,W 1gi..1a mCrum
:1. Who hu not sinned among men according to the Bible?
/\., Chrlat ..................................................... I Pt. 2:21-22

q, l1Chri1t holy or without sin because of His own power? A, Because He is made holy by the Father... Jn. 10:36
Conclusion: All the honors and great attributes of Christ
mentioned in the verses we have read are not indicative of His nature.

The nature of Christ
0- Despite the great attributes of Christ, what is His true nature, according to Jesus Himself?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Christ is a man according to Himself. Jn. 8:40
--- Q&A END ---
Note: In this instance Christ clearly introduced Himself as a man. Christ has never said that He is the true God. If He were the true God, He would have introduced Himself as such.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. According to His Apostles, what is the true nature of Christ?
A. 1) Man Mediator, according to
Apostle Paul ...................................... I Tim 2:5
2) Man, according to Apostle Peter Acts 2:22
3) Man, according to
Apostle Matthew ............................... Mt. 1: 18, 20
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Does the Bible teach that Christ has dual nature - that Heis true God and true man?
A. God is not man ....................................... Hos. 11:9
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Does God allow that man be God also?
A. Man is not God ....................................... Ezek. 28:2
--- Q&A END ---

r-h.1;,;LOn;I tl
"Ttn.,s. for cxarnple, it was.nol unUI 325 A.D., at: the Council of
Nic 1ca, thal the Church defined for us lhat it wasan article of faith that.
.Jesus is truly God."
\m::v.Clement.H. Crock, Discourses on the Apostles' Creed. Nihil Obstat: Arthur
J. Scanlan, Imprimatur: Patrick Cardinal Hayes. New York City: Joseph F. Wagner. Inc.,(c) 1938.]

--- Q&A START ---
Q. ls it surprising that after the time of the Apostles there would arise teachers who would preach a different Jesus, contrary to the one preached by the Apostles?
A. A warning was already given concerning
false teachers who would do this .......... II Cor. 11 :3-4
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
Therefore, the Bible teaches that we should honor and worship Christ, recognize Him as the Son of God, Savior, and the only Mediator between God and men, and the only holy man who has not committed sin. But, in spite of these attributes, the truth remains, as declared by our Lord Jesus Christ Himself. that He
is man.
(In the next lesson the Bible will show us the differences between God and Christ and the errors of those who teach that Christ is God in their manner of using the Bible.)



The Catholic invention
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who created the teaching that Christ is true God?
A. The Council of Nicaea in 325 A.O. .... A. Creed, p. 206 (24)
--- Q&A END ---






The Lord Jesus Christ
(Part II)

Theme: The distinctions between God and Christ and the biblical proofs that the true God is not Christ.

4) God does not get Is. 40:28 NIV

"Jacob"s well was there, and Jesus, tired as he was from the journey, sat down by the well "
(Bible. The Holy Bible, New International Version containing the Old Testament and the New Testament. New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.)



Proof
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What further proves that Christ is different from God?
A. Christ will be subjected to I Cor. 15:27-28
--- Q&A END ---








Introduction
If all people would rely solely on the Bible in their belief about the nature of Christ, no one would have fallen into the false belief that Christ is the true God. This is so because it is shown clearly in the Bible that God and Christ are two different beings.
Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What are the differences between God and Christ?
A. 1) God has no beginning Ps. 90:2
while Christ came from God ........ Jn. 8:42
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. When Christ was resurrected from the dead, what false impression did His disciples have of Him which our Lord Jesus Christ immediately corrected?
A. They thought He was a spirit
or God ...................................... ..... .. Lk. 24:36-37
Jn. 4:24
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Did Christ ever approve of His disciples' wrong notion that He is God? How did Christ show that He is different from God?
A. He proved that He is not a spirit or God but a man
with flesh and bones, the same man they knew before He died and was resurrected .. Lk. 24:38-39
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is it true that when Christ ascended into heaven He became
--- Q&A END ---

while Christ died .........................

0, God, then whyls He able to perform miracles?
