Q. What greater proof shows that the miracles performed by Christ cannot be used to validate the teachings that He is the true God?
A. Christ admitted:
"I can of my own self do nothing" ...... Jn. 5:30
--- Q&A END ---

(Part III)

Theme: Erroneous interpretations and wrong translations of biblical texts are the bases of those who teach that Christ is the true God.





--- Q&A START ---
Q. If Christ is not God, how did He rise from the dead? Did He resurrect Himself by His own power?
A. God resurrected Him Acts 2:23-24
--- Q&A END ---
Note: Christ's resurrection demonstrates that He died while the Bible proves that God is immortal. The Bible is clear on the fact that Christ was resurrected by God, the reason why He was brought back to life.

Conclusion
The clear distinctions between Christ and God prove that the true God is not Christ. Our Lord Jesus Christ possesses many attributes which surpass those of ordinary men but these do not show that Christ is God. The true God has attributes not found in Christ. If Christ did wonderful things like miracles, all these were done by God through Christ.
(In the next lesson we will study how biblical verses are misinterpreted and used erroneously by those who teach that Christ is God).




Introduction
Teachers of the "Christ is God" doctrine also use the Bible. They cite verses to prove their contention. But we assure you that if the biblical texts being used are correctly translated, there would be nothing in the Bible that shows Christ is the true God. We will examine now some of the verses used by these preachers and find out how they use such verses.
John 1:1, 14
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What biblical verse is given erroneous interpretation by the preachers of the "Christ is God" doctrine?
A. The Word was God who became man.. Jn. 1:1, 14
--- Q&A END ---
Note: The verse does not say Christ is the lrue God.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Does the Word refer to a pre-existent Christ? What does "the Word" refer to according to Catholic priests themselves?
A. A thought emanating from God ........... Jn. 1: 1 Douay (fn)
75
--- Q&A END ---

1e Fundamental .S.1'4/'a qf ttw lgleata nt Crt.sto
MSt.. John employs the t.enn Word. It ts so used only by St. John and the Son as a kind of intellectual emanation from the Father,"
[Utbl,r, 'l'hr. I loly Dible. Douay Version. Nth.ii Obstat: John M. Fearns, S.T.D., Imprimatur: Francis Cardinal Spellman, D.D. Ne York: C, Wildennann Co., Inc., IC) l915D.) .
N.Q.k: What the verse says is that the Word was with God. If the Word which was with God were God in nature, and the GodWhohad the Word was also God in nature, there would be two Gods. This contradicts biblical teachings.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Whyis it said that in the beginning the Word was with God?
A. Because Christ did not exist yet in the beginning though the idea concerning
Christ was already in God's mind. I Pt. 1:20 Douay
"Foreknown indeed before the foundation of the world, but manifested in the last times for you."
!Bible. The Holy Bible. Douay Version. Nihil Obstat: John M. Fearns, S.T.D., Imprimatur: Francis Cardinal Spellman, D.D. New York: C. Wildennann Co., Inc., (c) 1950.]
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion: Therefore, Christ had no existence in the ning but the idea concerning Him was already in the mind, or plan, of God, hence only a word.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. When did the Christ, who was only a plan or word in the beginning, come into existence?
A. When He was born of a woman ......... Gal. 4:4
Q. If it was the word which became flesh and not the God Who spoke the word, why does the text say the Word was God?
A. For no word of God is without power.. Lk. 1:37 ASV
"For no word from God shall be void of power"
[Bible. The Holy Bible, American Standard Version. Grand Rapids, Michigan: Christian Reformed Publishing House.]
Q. What does the Bible say concerning God and His power? Is God and His word of the same quality?
A. Almighty ........................................... Gen. 35: 11
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Thus, in the statement "The Word was God", the term "God" is not used as a noun but as an adjective. The term is used to describe the term "Word".

1,s Cl,r(st, 77

Wh 1t. does the statement "And the Wm-cl was made flesh"
mean?
--- Q&A START ---
A. The Word was fulfilled and its fulfillment
is a Mt. 1:18, 20
--- Q&A END ---

I John 5:20
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is Christ the true God mentioned in I John 5:20?
A. The true Godis the One introduced
by the Son ........................................ I Jn. 5:20
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why are we certain that Christ is not the true Godmentioned in I John 5:20?
A. No verse in the Bible states that Christ introduced Himself as
the true God.
--- Q&A END ---
Note: If Christ were the true God, there should have been a clear statement from Him that Heis introducing Himself as the true Godbecause, according to I John 5:20, the reason for the coming of the Son of God (Christ) is to introduce to the work). the true God. If He was not able to do this, then His coming would have been in vain. But we know that oneof the reasons of His coming was fulfilled because He was able lo introduce to the world who the true God is.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who is the true God introduced by Christ?
A. Toe Jn. 17:3, 1
--- Q&A END ---


FundanwntaL S.114/'1 qf tM nt Cruto
Romana 9:IS
q, What wrongly translated verse have the teachers of the "Christ 111 God" doctrine used?
A hlt;;S Cd l'orcver" Rom. 9:5 KN
"'Wt1o c:a. _n;th<.f: 1t;hers. and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ
ccrrTli;. wl'H.>IS all. God blessed for ever. Amen."
The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, translated out
, ,111'1'11;;original tongues and with the former translations diligently compared and rtivl::i<:d, set forth ln 1611 and commonly known as the King James Version, New 'Ycalc American Bible Society, 1970.]

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why do we say that this verse is wrongly translated?
A. The Father is the God who is above all.. Eph. 4:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. And who is the God blessed forever according to Apostle Paul?
A. The Father of our Lord Jesus II Cor. 1:3
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. So what is the correct translation of Romans 9:5?
A. In Romans 9:5 of the Revised Standard Version.
Christ is not the one referred to as God.. Rom. 9:5 RSV
"to them belong the patriarchs, and of their race, according to the flesh, is the Christ. God who is over all be blessed forever. Amen."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,(c) 1962]
--- Q&A END ---

Note: Iri the Revised Standard Version the part that says, "God who is over all be blessed for ever" - is written as a separate sentence. It is a doxology or praise which refers to our God, the Father.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Do other scholars of the Bible agree that this is the correct translation?
A. The majority accepts
the RSV rendering. The Interpreter's
Bible, Vol. IX, p. 540
'This half verse has been the center of interminable controversy. The issue appears from a comparison of our two English texts. Is God over all, blessed for ever or the one who is over all, Godblessed for ever) a phrase in apposition with MChrise and belonging in the same sentence as the rest ofvs. 5 (so the KJV and the RSV mg.). or is this phrase grammatically separate, a doxology to God at the end of the recital. of the privileges of Israel (so the RSV and most modem translators)? The question cannot be answered on the basis of the Greek since it 1,s a matter almost entirely of punctuation, and Greek MSS in the early period were not punctuated.
--- Q&A END ---

Tlw t"rcl Jesus Cnrtst 7
..... but the choice Is probably to be made between the KJV and lhe nsv l.rans.lations. The majority of modern commentators favor the latter because of the unlikelihood of Paul"s having here referred to
Christ as :·
IThe Interpreter's Bible. The Holy Scriptures in the King James and Revised Standard Versions with General Articles and Introduction, Exegesis, Exposition For Each Book of the Bible. In 1\velve Volumes. Nashville: Abingdon. Vol. IX. (Book))

Conclusion: Thus, Christ is not the one referred to as God in Romans 9:5. This verse as wrongly translated is being used as a basis by the teachers of the "Christ is God" doctrine.

Isaiah 9:6
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What other biblical passage is being given wrong interpretation by those who teach that Christ is God?
A. They say that Christ is the one referred
to as the Mighty Is. 9:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Whyis the claim that Christ is "the Mighty God" mentioned in Isaiah 9:6 a wrong interpretation?
A. Because what is called "Mighty God" in the verse is also being called
"Everlasting Is. 9:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who is being prophesied by Isaiah in this verse, a "Father" or a "Son"?
A. A son Is. 9:6(a)
--- Q&A END ---
Note: Since the prophesied one is not the Everlasting Father, hence, He is not the Mighty God.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. In that case, which is referred to as the "Mighty God" in Isaiah 9:6?
A. The government that would be
placed on His Is. 9:6
--- Q&A END ---

. What wrongly translated verse have the teachers of the
"Chrt11t is God" doctrine used?

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why do we say that this verse is wrongly translated?
A. The Father is the God who is above all .. Eph. 4:6
Q. And who is the God blessed forever according to Apostle Paul?
A. The Father of our Lord Jesus II Cor. 1:3
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. So what is the correct translation of Romans 9:5?
A. In Romans 9:5 of the Revised Standard Version,
Christ is not the one referred to as God.. Rom 9:5 RSV
"to them belong the patriarchs, and of their race, according to the flesh, is the Christ. God who is over all be blessed forever. Amen."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert 0. May and Bruce Metzger. NewYork: Oxford University Press,(c) 1962)
--- Q&A END ---

Note: In the Revised Standard Version the part that says, "God who is over all be blessed for ever" - is written as a separate sentence. It is a doxology or praise which refers to our God, the Father.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Do other scholars of the Bible agree that this is the correct translation?
A. The majority accepts .
the RSV rendering 1he Interpreter's
Bible, Vol. IX, p. 540
"This half verse has been the center of interminable controversy. The issue appears from a comparison of our two English texts. Is God over all, blessed for ever (or the one who is over all, God blessed for ever) a phrase in apposition with "Christ" and belonging in the same sentence as the rest ofvs. 5 (so the KJV and the RSV mg.), or is this phrase grammatically separate, a doxology to God at the end of the recital of the privileges of Israel (so the RSV and most modem translators)? The question cannot be answered on the basis of the Greek since it iiS a matter almost entirely of punctuation, and Greek MSS in the early period were not punctuated.
--- Q&A END ---

TM Lord. Chrtet 79
"... but the choice is probably to be made between the KJV and the RSV translations. The majority of modem commentators favor the latter because of the unlikelihood of Paul's having here referred to Christ as 'God' "
[The Interpreter's Bible. The Holy Scriptures in the King James and Revised Standard Versions with General Articles and Introduction, Exegesis, Exposition For Each Book of the Bible. In Twelve Volumes. Nashville: Abingdon. Vol. IX.
I
Conclusion: Thus, Christ is not the one referred to as God in Romans 9:5. This verse as wrongly translated is being used as a basis by the teachers of the "Christ is God" doctrine.

Isaiah 9:6
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What other verse of the Bible is being given wrong interpretation by those who teach that Christ is God?
A. They say that Christ is the one referred
to as the Mighty God ........................ Is. 9:6
Q. Is Christ the one referred to as the "Mighty God" in Isaiah 9:6?
A. The verse does not say that the child being prophesied will be called by the name "Mighty God". The child shall be called not by many names but by only one name. Hence, the verse says, "His name", and not "His names" as what is implied in the New King James version's rendition of the verse, where the terms "Wonderful, Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace" were mentioned individually. This is the reason others have mistakenly thought that the mention of "Mighty God" is one of the names by which the child will be called. Note: The name by which the child shall be called is mentioned after it has been stated, "And the government will be upon His shoulder." Therefore, the name refers to the government or office that will be given to the child, and not to His true nature.
Q. What is the proof that the expression "Mighty God" refers to the child's government or office and not to His true nature?
A. In the English rendition of the Old Testament by
the Jewish Publications Society of America, it is stated that the name, in the Hebrew language, is el-gibbor-Abi-ad-sar-shalom" ............ Is. 9:6 JPSA
--- Q&A END ---

.• , ) Jl\uutamentaL S."4(1 qf tlw n! Cristo
(J. Why 11 the government that would be placed on the
¦ houldere of Christ called Mighty God and Everlasting Father?
I\. ,Because this came from the Father ... Mt. 28:18

--- Q&A START ---
A. According to prophet Isaiah himself, what is the nature of the one he prophesied in Isaiah 9:6?
A A man of sorrows s. 53:3
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Thus, the teachers of the "Christ is God" doctrine give wrong meanings or interpretations to these verses as they use them in their teachings. We should not allow them to deceive us.

The Holy Spirit
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is the Holy Spirit God as taught by the Bible?
A. No, but is sent by God ...................... Jn. 14:26
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who else sends the Holy Spirit?
A. Christ also sends the Holy Jn. 15:26
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How many spirits are sent by God?
A. He sends seven spirits ...................... Rev. 5:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the source of the teaching that the Holy Spirit is God?
A. The Council of Constantinople .......... Apos. Creed, p. 206
"In 381, at the Council of Constantinople, it was defined that it is an article of faith that the Holy Ghost is God."
[Rev. Clement H. Crock, Discourses on the Apostles' Creed. Nihil Obstat: Arthur
J. Scanlan, Imprimatur: Patrick Cardinal Hayes. New York City: Joseph F. Wagner, Inc.. (c) 1938. (Book)]
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
Therefore, we should not allow ourselves to be deceived by the teachers who say Christ is the true God even if they use biblical verses to back up their contentions. This is because we cannot read from the Bible any verse which states that Christ affirms He is the true God. Why then are they able to use some biblical verses? These verses are given erroneous meanings or false interpretations by false teachers or they are translated incorrectly.




LESSON NO. 17
bifii4iii&&k m

Leading a New Life
Theme: It is the obligation of the members of the Church of Christ to lead a :riew life.

Introduction
To attain salvation, it is not enough that a person simply joins and becomes a member of the Church of Christ. Our Savior Jesus Christ demands that we follow the fundamental change in way oflife He has set up for us. This renewal is the subject of the present lesson.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What distinctive characteristic should be possessed by a member of the Church of Christ?
A. A new creature - the old has
passed away ..................................... II Car. 5:17
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which old things should be left behind so that onebecomes a new creature as a member of the Church of Christ?
--- Q&A END ---
81

B2 Jl\lnr:lanwrUII... 4/rlw lglalamCNeo
--- Q&A START ---
A. Abandon the old aelf that la corrupt... Eph. 4:21-22
Q. What 1hould members of the Church of Christ put on after
--- Q&A END ---
leaving the former evil ways?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Be renewed In the spirit of their minq. Eph. 4:23-24
Imtruction You should lead a completely new life.
Q. Why is it wrong for a member of the Church of Christ to continue living in evil?
A. Because the unrighteous will not
inherit the kingdom of God ............... . I Cor. 6:9-10
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Are they not in the Church of Christ? How come they will not inherit the Kingdom of God?
A. They shall be cast out into the world
when the darkness comes Job 18:5, 18
--- Q&A END ---

Obligation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What should we do with the works of the flesh?
A. Put them to death or
do away with Col. 3:5-9
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What are these works of the flesh that should be suppressed or avoided?
A. Enumerate the works of the Gal. 5:19-21 Instruction: If you used to get drunk or you used to drink Uquor or take drugs, you should; from now on, refrain from indulging in any of these vices. God forbids them.
If you used to gamble, you should know that allforms of gambling are prohibited in the Church of Christ, like racing, lotteries, card or dice games, slot machines, etc.
If you are involved in an adulterous relationship, you should stop this right away. God forbids adultery.
If you are unlawfully employed, committing fraud or any illegal acts, this is not allowed. We should live decently and righteously.
You should cease from doing any vice or sin in your life. Refrain from swearing or using foul language and do not perform the kind.of dances popul ed intheworld,
terized by provocative and sensuous behavior.
--- Q&A END ---

L,_x1dl1t1/ 11 N;J,11Uf,,

--- Q&A START ---
Q. For this reason, what should a person do before he becomes a member of the Church of Christ?
A. He should bear fruits worthy of
repentance ........................................ Mt. 3:8, 10
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Should one hide or deny his sins? What should we do to our sins?
A. Confess and declare them to God Ps. 32:5
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can one prove his sincere desire to return to God?
A. By abandoning the former evil ways .. Is. 55:7
--- Q&A END ---

Promise
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What blessing will be received by those who repent or who confess their si:Qs to God?
A. They will be forgiven and cleansed .... I Jn. 1:9
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What if the sins we have committed in the past are serious ones? Can they still be forgiven?
A. No matter howgrave the sin Is. 1:18
--- Q&A END ---

Warning
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is God:s warning to those who refuse to lead a new life?
A. They will be Is. 1:19-20
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
We have learned that it is notenough just tobein the Church of Christ; one should undergo a complete renewal of Ufe. The member who has turned a newlife truly belongs to Christ and to God. Put into practice the commandments we have received.





Worship Services
Th,ern,e: It is the responsibility of the members of the Church of Christ to attend worship services on the days set for them.

Introduction
Twice a week, members of the Church of Christ hold worship services. Such congregational worship is conducted in the chapel or house of worship. We should give great value to this duty and not disregard or neglect it. On any day that a worship service is set by the Church, weshould attend it as it is our duty to worship God.
Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why is it the duty of all members of the Church of Christ to worship God?
A. This is sought by the Father ............. Jn. 4:23
--- Q&A END ---
Note: God our Father seeks the true worshippers. There are, of course, worshippers who are not true. The true worshippers are those who have the right and privilege to worship God because they have been redeemed or cleansed
84

nrp. 85
by the blood of Christ. and these are the members of the Church of Christ.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What power has God to require us to worship Him?
A. He created us and we are His people... Ps. 95:6- 7
--- Q&A END ---
Note: It is really the obligation of all people created by God to worship Him as their Creator. But because the world has turned away from God, the ones expected by God to render Him true worship are the members of the Church of Christ, who are His people in these last days.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Where do the people of God worship Him?
A. In His holy temple ............................. Ps. 5:7
Explain: The temple referred to in the verse is equivalent to our present chapel or house of worship.
Q. Which is this worship performed in the house of worship and how is this done?
A. This is the gathering together with
singing, prayers, and I Cor. 14:26, 15 Explain: The program of our congregational worship is not something new. This is also the program of congregational worship of the first Christians. They had prayers, singings of hymns. and studies of God's words.
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the Apostle's strict instruction concerning our gathering together as the members of the Church to worship God? .
A. Do not forsake Heb. 10:25 Conclusion: Thus, no one can insist that he will just perform his worship to God in his own house because the strict instruction is not to forsake the assembling of ourselves together or our attendance at gatherings.
--- Q&A END ---


Hi; 11\mdamental Bel q/tM nf Crtsto
q. When did the flrat Church perfonn their gathering together
or worship services?
--- Q&A START ---
A. On the first day of the Acts 20:7
--- Q&A END ---

J. Why 1B IL th;;fl, in the Church of Christ, aside from Sunday, thoretrc other days set also for worship seivices? Were there S Lhal the first Church performed worship seivices on otJJer days aside from the first day of the week?
A Tl:.H:Te was a time when they worshipped
everyday Acts 2:46

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What authority does the Church Administration have in setting the day for worship seIVices?
A. Confirmed in heaven ............................. Mt. 18:19 Explain: The Church Administration has the authority to decide on matters concerning the seIVice of God, such as setting the days for worship seivices. Their decision is confirmed by God in heaven. This is why, ifwe neglect such seIVices, we will not be simply accountable to man but to God Who seeks us to worship Him.
Obligation
Q. What if we have to transfer residence for reasons of livelihood? What is God's strict instruction to us?
A. Look for the house of God wherever it is.. Deut. 12:5 Instruction: Wherever we go, we should look first for the house of worship. Wherever we may be, we should bear in mind that wearemembers of the Church ofChrist and people of God. Therefore, He expects us to devote time for worshipping Him.
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is God's warning to those who neglect worship seivices?
A. He will punish them in the lake of fire... Heb. 10:25-27 E:,g>lain: To neglect the worship seIVice is considered a serious sin. God will inflict terrible punishment on those who do so. From now on never miss attendance, unless you are seriously ill and physically incapable.
Q. Will Christ accept the various excuses offered by those who neglect worship seIVices?
--- Q&A END ---

n,p 87
--- Q&A START ---
A. Those who offer excuses are not worthy
of the kingdom of Lk. 9:59-62 Instruction: You should not be hindered by your employment or profession or any personal interest in performing this duty. God should be given priority over anything or anyone in the world.
--- Q&A END ---

Worship God in proper attire
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How should each one of us present ourselves before God whenever we gather together to worship Him?
A. With Ps. 89:7
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why should we present ourselves in our worship of God in
--- Q&A END ---
the house of the Lord with utmost reverence?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Holiness befits the house
of the Lord Ps. 93:5 NASB
"Thy testimonies are fully confirmed; Holiness befits Thy house,0
LoRD, forevermore."
[Bible. New American Standard Bible. New York and Cleveland: Colliers Publishers,(c) 1960, 1962, 1963, 1968, 1971, 1972, 1973.[
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why should we strictly obseive reverence and holiness during the worship seivice? To whom is this reverence rendered?
A. The Lord is in His holy Habak. 2:20 TEV
"The LoRD is in his holy Temple; let everyone on earth be silent in his presence."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Stucly Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.[
--- Q&A END ---

Note: According to this verse, everyone should be silent in His presence: thus, worship in the Church of Christ is a solemn occasion. We do not behave in an unruly manner. We do not engage in useless conversation; neither do we come and go anywhere at leisure. And this orderliness should be maintained.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is this command still in effect during the Christian era? Isn't it out of date? What did the Apostles teach to preseive the solemnity of the worship seivice?
--- Q&A END ---

l'l'l',e Interpreter's Dictionary oft:he Bible. Vol. I, p. 231)

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is proper attire?
A. Holy array - garments that are clean and fitting for the sacred occasion Ps. 96:9
Interpreter's Bible Commentary
--- Q&A END ---

Ps. 96:9
"Worship the LORD in holy array; tremble before him, all the

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Whal is one of the things that ought to be obseived in a holy. proper, and orderly worship service?
A. Worship Him in holy array Ps. 96: 1-3 & 8-9 RSV
"O sing to the LORD a new song; sing to the LORD, all the earth! Sing to the LORD, bless his name; tell of his salvation from day to day. Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples!
"Ascribe to the LORD the glory due his name; bring an offering, and come into his courts! Worship the LORD in holy array; tremble before him, all the earth!"
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. Mayand Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press, Inc.,(c) 1962.)
--- Q&A END ---

Note: Holy array during worship service is part of the command of orderliness. Ifwe obey the command to sing, to pray, to give offerings, and to enter the house of worship, we should also, in like manner, obey the command to be in holy array.
We do not present ourselves to our fellowmen in underwear. The kind of attire we wear when we ourselves before a person indicates the degree of respect we have for that person. If for other people we show our respect in the kind of clothing we wear, the more we should show respect in the attire we wear whenever we present ourselves before God in the worship service.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What does the expression "holy array" mean in the context of the verse?
A. An expression indicating
proper attire The Interpreter's
Diet. of the Bible, Vol. I, p. 231
--- Q&A END ---

earth!"

Commentary
"Worship... in holy array, i.e., garments that are ritually clean and fitting for the sacred occasion."
[The Interpreter's Bible. The Holy Scriptures in the King James and Revised Standard Versions with General Articles and Introduction, Exegesis, Exposition For Each Book of the Bible. In Twelve Volumes. Nashville: Abingdon. Vol. IV)

Note: The attire we wear should befit the sacredness of the occasion. Worship service is a formal occasion and it calls for formal attire. There are still others who have not yet recognized the sanctity of our congregational gathering with due respect. Teaching us the things that weshould do is part of preparing us to be presentable to God.
Promise
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is Christ's prom.ise in our worship services?
A. He will be with us in
our gatherings ...... .. .. .. ........ .. .. ........ .. Mt. 18:20
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can Christ be with us in our worship services?
A. By means of the Spirit Gal. 4:6
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the value of the Spirit's dwelling in us?
A. This strengthens our spiritual life and we will
be firm in our love Eph. 3:16-18 TEV
"I ask God from the wealth of his glory to give you power thsou.gh hi.s Spirit to be strong in your inner selves, so that you, together with all God's people, may have the power to understand how broad ancl long, how high and deep, is Christ's love."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.)
--- Q&A END ---

IQ, l\utdanwnlal.._qfdlei,.fa"' ere.tu
Great will be received from God by who attend worship services because the Splrtt Who 1trengt.hene their spiritual life dwells In them and confirms
them In their love.



[LESSON N 0. 19



Our Voluntary Contributions

Conclusion
We believe that attendance at worship services is an obligation to God. We receive great blessings from worship services because we experience the Spirit of Christ Who strengthens and confirms us.


Theme: It is God's command that we give butions and Thanksgiving offerings.
















Introduction
One ofthe obligations ofthemembers ofthe Church of Christ is the giving of contributions and thanksgiving offerings. But we must understand clearly that the giving of contributions by members of the Church is not in any way similar to the giving of alms to beggars. Ifwe hear some people criticizing our giving of contributions and thanksgiving offerings, we must realize that these are but attempts to destroy our belief. We do such not because the ministers command it on their own authority but because this is based on the truth written in the Bible.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the basis of our giving of contributions?
A. God's command that we
should not forget ... .................. .... ..... Heb. 13:J 5-16 RSV
"Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God, that is, the fruit of lips that acknowledge his name. Do not
--- Q&A END ---

91


R2 P'undamP.ntal 13P.ltf!f." qfthP. lglP.sta nl Cristo
nr"lect to do "ood nnd to share what you have, for ,mch sacriflces are pleai.ln to God,"
llltblr.. '111r Oxforcl Aunotatcd Utble. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. by llrrhr.rt. G. Muy und Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University J'rr.Nf'I, 0 J 962.)

N,Qk: The command to give contributions is written in the Bible and is not a man-made commandment preached by the ministers of the Church. If this is fulfilled by His servants, God will be truly pleased.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Whom does God command to give contributions and how should this be fulfilled?
A. Each one should give as his heart
so desires ......................................... II Car. 9:7
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: It is the duty of every member of the Church of Christ to give contributions. We should do this edly and cheerfully knowing that this truly pleases God.

Obligation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Howshould weregard our act of giving contributions? Should we liken this to the act of giving alms to beggars?
A. This is a load or an obligation Gal. 6:5
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the proof showing that the giving of contributions is one of our loads or obligations?
A. Those who are taught the word should
share with him who Gal. 6:6 RSV
"Let him who is taught the word share allgood things with him who teaches."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can we share with or help those who teach the Gospel?
A. By means of contributions. II Car. 9:12-13 RSV
"For the rendering of this service not only supplies the wants of the saints but also overflows in many thanksgivings to God. Under the test of this service, you will glorify God by your obedience in acknowledging the gospel of Christ, and by the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others."
--- Q&A END ---

OurVolwwut1 Contrtbuttons 93
[Bfb!e. Toe Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version.
ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.)

Explain: Giving contributions is an obligation or responsibility to God. To fail to do this is to commit a sin.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How should we prepare our contributions?
A. Set a ide an amount in proportion
to what one has I Car. 16:2 TEV
"Every Sunday each of you must put aside some money, in proportion to what he has earned. and save it up, so that there will be no need to collect money when I come."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

Instruction: We should prepare beforehand our butions. We should set aside a certain portion of our income for this. We should not give just what is in excess or what is left of our income after budgetting for our needs but set aside our contributions in the same manner we set aside for our needs. Considering the fact that this is for God. we should consider it more important than the rest of our needs.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why should we prepare beforehand or set aside the amount for our contributions?
A. To make it voluntary and not forced -
hence the term contribution ............. II Car. 9:5
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What are the uses for the contributions given by the members of the Church of Christ?
A. For the propagation of the Gospel
and for the needs of the II Car. 9:12-13
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What else are our contributions used for?
A. Construction of houses of Hag. 1:8 Conclusion: The contributor is the first beneficiary or his contribution. By means of his contributions, worship services are held, the message of God is delivered, and worship of God is done in well-constructed and decent houses of worship.
--- Q&A END ---

M 1111/, 11,.. rn 1n

W'l1y should we not be offended or tight-fisted in giving contributions?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Whal. we give really belongs to God... I Chron. 29:11-12
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Thus, what does God expect from us?
A. Joy and willingness to give Chron. 29:9
Q. Do economic hardships prevent the true servants of God from giving contributions?
A. Despite economic difficulties, they are generous in their giving and they even
give more than they can .................... II Cor. 8: 1-3 TEV
"Our brothers, we want you to know what God's grace has accomplished in the churches in Macedonia. They have been severely f'ested by the troubles they went through; but their joy was so great that they were extremely generous in their giving. even though they are very poor. I can assure you that they gave as much as they could, and even more than they could. Of their own free will"
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Toctay's English Version. Catholic Study Ectition. Nashville. U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion: Thus, poverty is not an excuse to God for failure to give contributions. Poverty of even greater proportions were suffered by the early Christians, but they still gave contributions. The same thing is being done by many members of the Church of Christ today.
The Offering of Thanksgiving
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why do we still have Thanksgiving with offerings at the end of every year?
A. God's Ps. 50:14
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is thanksgiving still commanded in the Christian era? And why is thanksgiving also done during the anniversary of the Church?
--- Q&A END ---

Our Voluntary Contributions !)5

--- Q&A START ---
A. It is God's will to give thanks
in I Thess. 5:18
--- Q&A END ---
Note: lt is commanded that we give thanks for everything. The anniversary of the Church is a great thing for us and hence an occasion to be thankful for.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What do we render unto Godwhen we offer our thanksgiving to Him?
A. Giving th ks honors Ps. 50:23 TEV
"Giving thanks is the sacrifice that honors me, and 1 will surely save all who obey me."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How is thanksgiving done?
A. By means of praise I Chron. 16:8-9 NN
"Give thanks to the LORD, call on his name; make known among the nations what he has done. Sing to him, sing praise to him; tell of all his wonderful acts."
[Bibte. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the Old Testament and the New Testament. New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.I
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why do we still bring offerings on thanksgiving?
A. It is commanded to bring offerings .... I Chron. 16:29 Conclusion: Thus, on thanksgiving day, whether at the end of the year or the anniversary of the Church, we hold special worship services to praise God and to bring offerings.
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How should we prepare our offerings for thanksgiving?
A. For a period of one II Cor. 9:2
--- Q&A END ---

:1{ q/ttw t,i.,ta nt Crtato
1. Why should we prepare our offering in this manner?
/\. To be ready as a matter of generosity
and not as a grudging obltgatlon ....... II Cor. 9:5 lnatructtop: The easiest way to prepare our offering for thankeglving is by weekly savings. (Teach how this is done.)

Promise
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is God's promise to those who contribute and give offerings?
--- Q&A END ---
A,. God can pour abundant blessings .... II Cor. 9:7-8 Emphasize: The brethren who set aside something for their offering to God do not lose anything nor do they stand tolose, because God has prepared for them blessings even in this life.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What great blessing awaits those who give offerings?
A. The true life I Tim. 6:18-19 NW
"Command them to do good. to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share. In this waythey will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age, so that they may take hold of the life that is truly life."
[Dible, The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the OldTestament and the New Testament. New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.I
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion
Thus, we should believe that we, members of the Church of Christ, have obligations to give contributions and offerings. We should do this wholeheartedly, freely and gladly. But, to enable us to give our offerings in this manner, weshould set them aside. We should not be offended in doing this because God commanded such and the amount that wegive really comes from Him. Most of all, ifwe contribute, we are laying up a foundation for the attainment of true life.



LESSON N 0. 20


The Brotherhood
Theme: God commands that the brethren should love one another.

Introduction
Members of the Church of Christ have a very unique tionship with one another. They constitute a brotherhood lished by Christ and willed by God. Thus, the members of the Church should associate and relate with one another in accordance with the teachings of Christ so that the brotherhood He envisioned will be fulfilled in the Church He founded.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the Bible's teaching regarding the brotherhood in I.he Church?
A. Love the brotherhood Pt. 2:1 7
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why is it vital to love the brotherhood? What. is the relevance of our love for the brethren to our relationship to Christ?
A. The true disciple of Christ loves
his Jn. 13:34-35
--- Q&A END ---

97

Jl\mdamenml Bel qf tha lglesfa nt Cristo
, Why 11 the penon who loves hts brethren in the Church
recognized u a true disciple of Christ?
I\, He made us all brothers Mt. 23:8
Concluaton: Therefore, you should regard all members of the
Church, including ministers, as your brothers (or sisters, as the case may be). Let us treat one another as equals within the Church.

The Method
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How should we practice our love for the brethren?
A. Do not withhold help from those
in need I Jn. 3:17 NW
"If anyqne has material possessions and sees his brother in need but has no pity on him, how can the love of God be in him?"
[Bible. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the Old Testament and the New Testament. New York: International Bible Society, (c) 1978.)
--- Q&A END ---

Instrnction: We should bear with one another. Let us help the less fortunate brethren and let us not withhold from them whatever good things we can do for them.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who among the brethren do not deserve our help?
A. Those who do not live according to the teaching
but rather lead disorderly lives ...... .. . II Thess. 3:6 NW
"In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, we command you, brothers, to keep away from every brother who is idle and does not live according to the teaching you received from us."
[Bible. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the Old Testament and the New Testament. New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who are those who lead disorderly lives?
A. Those who do not work or those
who refuse to earn a living ........... ..... II Thess. 3:11-12
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why should we not help those who are lazy or those who refuse to work or earn a living?
A. Such people do not deserve to eat ..... II Thess. 3:10
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How can we safeguard the brotherhood in the Church?
A. Grudge not one against Js. 5:9 KJV
--- Q&A END ---

'1'I u.; LJrul.l LCrl UJLJCl

nol Ol1.C againsl.another, brethren, les1 ye be condemned: beholcl. the ,i udge sl.andeth before the door."
[Bible. The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, translated out of the original tongues and with the former translations diligently compared and revised, set forth in 1611 and commonly known as the King James Version, New York: American Bible Society, 1970.)

Instruction: This grudging against each other is the work of the people of the world and not of the true disciples of Christ. We should not do it.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Supposing a brother or a sister has done us wrong, what is forbidden ofus to safeguard the brotherhood in the Church?
A. Do not return evil for evil .. .. .. ..... .. .. .. . I Pt. 3:8-9 RSV
"Finally, all of you, have unity of spirit, sympathy, love of the brethren, a tender heart and a humble mind. Do not return evil for evil or reviling for reviling; but on the contrary bless, for to this you have been called, that you may obtain a blessing."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What else is forbidden of us so that the brotherhood in the Church may be always upheld?
A. Stop evil tongues and speak no guile.. I Pt.. 3: 10 Instruction: We should not speak evil things against the brethren in the Church. We should not destroy their reputation.
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What should not be done to anyone especially to our brethren in the Church?
A. We should not defraud anyone Cor. 6:8
--- Q&A END ---
Instruction: We should not do what others do who borrow without returning what they borrowed or asking for a loan of money and never paying it back. We should avoid doing evil to brethren in the Church.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. If a brother or a sister in the Church commits evil against us. what should we not do against him or her?
A. Suing him or her in a civil court
is forbidden ......... .. ..... ...... .. ....... .. ..... I Cor. 6:6 TEV
--- Q&A END ---

JO() Fundamental lJ,rl s qf the nt Cristo
"ln11leml, one Christian goes to court against. another and 1111hrllevrr'4 Juclgr the case!"
IH!hln, Oood N11wK IJ!blr., The Bible In Today's English Version, Catholic Study cll11on, Nm1hvlllr., U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]

Inst.met.ton: If somehow a brother or a sister in the faith has done us wrong, we should not sue him or her ip court. God does not allow us to do this.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What should we do instead of suing the brother or sister in a civil court?
A. Bring the case to the Church I Cor. 6:4 TEV
"If such matters come up, are you going to take them to be settled by people who have no standing in the church?"
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. NashvilJe, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion: If conflicts arise among us, the proper thing to do to settle or resolve them is to bring them to the attention of the Administration of the Church.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Therefore, what does Christ ask us to do first whenever a brother or a sister sins against us?
A. Go to him or her and talk privately ... Mt. 18: 15 TEV
"If your brother sins against you, go to him and show him his fault. But do it privately, just between yourselves. If he listens to you, you have won your brother back."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

Note: The offended party is told by Christ to approach the offender. We are not told to avoid whoever offends us, much less to detest him or her. Christ commands instead that we talk privately first with those who offend us.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What should be done if he refuses to hear us?
A. Bring one or two others as witnesses.. Mt. 18:16 TEV
"But if he will not listen to you, take one or two other persons with you, so that 'every accusation may be upheld by the testimony of two or more witnesses,' as the scripture says."
--- Q&A END ---

The Brotherhood 101
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible In Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]

Instruction: If he or she does not listen to you, you may bring an elder or two in the Church to serve as witnesses in your talk for the sake of reconciliation. Do this instead of going to court.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Should he or she refuse to listen, what should be done?
A. Tell it to the Church, and if he or she still refuses to listen, consider him
or her a Gentile ................................ Mt. 18:17 RSV
"Ifhe refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church; and ifhe refuses to listen even to the church, let him be to you as a Gentile and a tax collector."
!Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.]
--- Q&A END ---

Instruction: If despite the steps that you have taken, he or she still refuses to be reconciled, bring the matter to the Church Administration. If he or she still refuses to listen to the Church Administration, Christ tells us to regard him or her as a Gentile.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who are referred to as "Gentiles"?
A. The Gentiles are separated
from Christ.. ..................................... Eph. 2:11(a), 12
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Thetefore, he or she who refuses to be reconciled with his or her brethren in the Church, in spite of the mediation of the Church Administration, will be expelled from the Church.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Thus, what then is the obligation of the brethren in Lhe Church who have conflict with each other?
A. Be reconciled at once Mt. 5:23-26 RSV
"So if you are offering your gift at the altar, and there rernernber that your brother has something against you, leave your gi.ll there before the altar and go; first be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift. Make friends quickly with your accuser, while you are going with him to court, lest your accuser hand you over to I.he judge, and the judge to the guard, and you be put in prison; truly, 1 say to you, you will never get out till you have paid the last penny."
--- Q&A END ---

(nl. 1111, ly/J.:su;, 11/ c,t
IBfbi., The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert 0. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press, 01 82,1
Instruction: We should not allow ourselves to go on
harboring hatred or anger against anyone. It does not mean we should not attend worship services while we are still angcy with someone but that weshould be reconciled at once with him or her.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How should those who are angry with each other reconcile?
A. Forgive each Eph. 4:32
--- Q&A END ---


I LESSON No. 21
@ Ą# .

Other Prohibitions
Theme: God forbids the eating of blood and intermarriage with a nonmember.





Warning
--- Q&A START ---
Q. How evil is the act of harboring hatred for the brethren?
A. This is equivalent to committing
murder.. ........................................... I Jn. 3:15
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Where do murderers abide?
A. Indeath Jn.3:14
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Whoever harbors hatred against his or her brethren is in danger of damnation.

Conclusion
We must observe and safeguard the brotherhood in the Church not only by means of calling each other "brother" or "sister" but more so by love and concern for_our brethren's welfare. We should be reconciled at once with whomever we have differences and we should not harbor hatred against anyone.


Introduction
Members of the Church of Christ are prohibited from eating blood and from contracting marriage with those of other faiths. These prohibitions are criticized by our detractors especially in the Philippines where the eating of blood and mixed marriages are commonplace. We are undaunted, however. in observing such prohibitions because we know too well who their author is.
Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who forbids the eating of blood and since when has this prohibition been enforced?
A. God has forbidden it since
the time of Noah Gen. 9:1-4
--- Q&A END ---
Note: God stated clearly which things may be eaten and which should not be eaten. He forbids the eating of blood.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Has this prohibition continued to be enforced until the Christian era?
--- Q&A END ---
103

l-'111 I 1, 111 Crt.s
--- Q&A START ---
A., IJo 110'1 unl l)loocl .............................. Acts 21:25 TEV
"But aa for the Gentiles who have become believers, we have sent t.hem a letter telling them we decided that they must not eat any fopd I hat. has been offered to idols, or any blood, or any animal that has been strangled, and that they must keep themselves from sexual Immoral!t.y."
(IJtble. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

The Reason
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why does God forbid the eating of blood?
A. Blood is Deut. 12:22-23
--- Q&A END ---
Note: Blood is not given as food.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. For what did God reserve blood?
A. For the forgiveness of sins
and redemption of life ..... .... ....... ....... Lev. 17:11 TEV
"The life of every living thing is in the blood and that is whythe LoRD has commanded that all blood be poured out on the altar to take away I.he people's sins. Blood, which is life, takes away sins."
[Bible. Good News Bible, The Bible in Today's English Version, Catholic Study Edition. Nashville, U.S.A.: Catholic Bible Press,(c) 1979.]
--- Q&A END ---

Explain: During the time of the Israelites, they offered the blood of animals on the altar to atone for their sins. The purpose for which God intended blood to be used is holy and not for purposes of eating.
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What was being foreshadowed by the blood of animals being offered by the people of Israel?
A. They foreshadowed the good things
lo come ... .. .. .. . . .. . . .. . .. ..... .. ..... ....... .. .... Heb. 10:1
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is ibis good thing foreshadowed by the offering of animal blood by the people of Israel?
A. The redemption by Christ by means
of His Heb. 9:13-14
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Thus, what does God command us ifwe slaughter animals for food?
A. Pour out the blood and cover it
withdust Lev.17:12-13
--- Q&A END ---

Other ProhtbttfDns 105

Instruction: In preparing or dressing fowls and animals prior to cooking, make sure to pour out the blood.

Warning
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What evil is committed before God by a person who eats blood?
A. He becomes God's enemy
and is Lev. 17:10
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Therefore, we should nowstay away and refrain from eating blood or whatever food mixed with blood if we do not want to become an enemy of God and be expelled from His people.

On Marriage
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why is a member of the Church of Christ: forbidden to intermarry with someone from another religion?
A. God forbids II Cor. 6: 14-15 Lamsa
"Do not unite in marriage with unbelievers, for what fellowship has righteousness with iniquity? Or what mingling has light. with darkness? Or what accord has Christ with Satan? Orwhat portion has a believer with an unbeliever?"
--- Q&A END ---

[Bible. The Holy Bible from Ancient Eastern Manuscripts; Containing the Oki ,tncl New Testaments Translated from the Peshitt.a, The Authorized Bible or I.he
Church of the East. by George M. Lamsa. Philadelphia: A.J. Holman,@ 1.957. "Reprinted under Authority of Presidential Decree No. 285 as amended by PD 400."]

--- Q&A START ---
Q. When did God start prohibiting His servants from marrying anyone who is not from His own people?
A. In the olden Deut. 7:3-4
--- Q&A END ---
Note: Even engagement with anyone who is not a member of His people is also forbidden.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is it a small sin against God to marry a nonmember?
A. A great Neh. 13:27, 26
--- Q&A END ---

,,j fql,: 'Jf,, tu

,J, What wlll we lose if we love the world?
/\. Tlrn lov of the l?'ather .. ... .. .. .. .. .. .. ... I Jn. 2: 15
Conduston: Therefore, it is such a great sin for a member of the Church of Christ to marry a nonmember. This will mean losing God's love for us.



L E S S O N N 0. 22


On Baptism
Theme: Baptism is necessary for one to become a disciple of Christ.





--- Q&A START ---
Q. Is it right for a member of the Church of Christ to elope with the one he or she wants to marry?
A. This is a sin against God because this
is an insult to the parents Mt. 15:4
--- Q&A END ---
Instruction: If you desire to get married, show your respect for your parents by informing them of your intentions to do so. Do not elope just toget married. Be sure toget permission from your parents. Just courting or being engaged with a non-member is already prohibited by God. Those of you who are still single, should not marry an unbeliever.
You are also commanded to avoid joining organizations or labor unions whose principles run in conflict with biblical teachings.












Introduction
Although many of those who become members of the Church of Christ have been baptized already in their former religions, they should realize that thebaptism they received is not the kind commanded by our Lord Jesus Christ: those who administered it have no authority to preach and to baptize. Hence. those baptized by them have not received the blessings associated with the baptism commanded by Christ.

Presentation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What should be done to those who are being made disciples, according to Christ's command?
A. Baptize them .................................... Mt. 28: 19 RSV
"Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit."
[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. May and Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.]
--- Q&A END ---

Note: For one to become a disciple of Christ, he cannot avoid being baptized.

107

JO, l"undtuMntal .s.1'4(1 qfttw nl Cristo
g, What other ble11tng le received In the true baptism?
--- Q&A START ---
A. ForgtveneH of etne Acts 2:38
--- Q&A END ---


O,1.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Thus, where is the true baptism done?
A. Where there is plenty of water Jn. 3:23 NW
--- Q&A END ---


109

g, What did Christ assure those who receive the true baptism?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Mk. 16:15-16
--- Q&A END ---
Conclusion: Therefore, it is vital that we receive the true
baptism so that not only will we become disciples of Christ
but also have our sins forgiven and attain salvation.

On the mode of Baptism
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Which baptism commanded by Christ did the Apostles preach?
A. Baptism unto death ....... .... ... .......... .. Rom 6:3 NW
"Or don't you know that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptised into his death?"
[Bible. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the OldTestament and the New Testament, New York: International Bible Society, (c) 1978.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why is the baptism preached by the Apostles called baptism unto death?
A. We have been united
with him in His death ....................... Rom. 6:5 NW
"If we have been united with him like this in his death, we will certainly also be united with him in his resurrection."
[Bible. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the Old Testament and the New Testament, New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.]
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. How do we become united with our Lord Jesus Christ in His death?
A. By means of Rom. 6:4 NW
--- Q&A END ---

"We were therefore buried with him through baptism into death in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too may live a new life."
[Bible. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing the OldTestament and the New Testament, New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.]

Note: Those who receive the true baptism are "buried" according to Apostle Paul. Therefore baptism is likened to a burial. Thus the person baptized is immersed in water as though he is being buried.


"Now John also was baptising at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water, and people were constantly coming to be baptised."

[Bible. The Holy International Version containing the OldTestament and the NewTestament, New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.]

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why is plenty of water needed for the performance of the true baptism?
A. Because both the one baptizing and the person to be baptized will go
into the water ·..·.. Acts 8:38
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Do Catholic authorities agree that the true baptism is conferred or done by immersion?
A. Even the Catholic priests agree
that it should be so ......................... Cath. Ency., Vol. II
p. 261-262
"The very word 'baptize', as we have seen, means a washing The
most ancient form usually employed was unquestionably immersion. This is not only evident from the writings of the Fathers and the early rituals of both the Latin and Oriental Churches, but it can also be gathered from the Epistles of St. Paul, who speaks of baptism as a bath (Eph. v, 26; Rom. vi, 4; 'Fit., iii, 5). In the Latin Church, imme:rsion seems to have prevailed until the twelfth century."
[The Catholic Encyclopedia, Edited by Charles G. Herbermann. New York: Encyclopedia Press, 1913.]
--- Q&A END ---

Conclusion: Thus, in the Church of Christ, baptism is done by immersion in water because this is the manner taught by the Bible and even Catholic priests agree that this is so.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why is the baptism done in the Catholic Church different from what the Bible teaches?
A. They changed it Faith Of Our Fathers
p. 228 (213)
"For several centuries after the establishment of Christianity Baptism was usually conferred by immersion; but since the t welft.h century the practice of baptizing by infusion has prevailed in the Catholic Church, as this manner is attended with less inconveni.ence than Baptism by immersion."
--- Q&A END ---


no q/tM lgr.aftl "' Cream
Cardinal Olbborw, ffw .Pufth q/Our Fatlwra, New York: P.J. Kenedy &
Iona, o 1117, IBookJI
g, What 11 wrong if we change the commandment of God by
addin& to or subtracting from what He has commanded?
--- Q&A START ---
A. Adding to or subtracting from His
commandments is sin ....................... Deut. 12:32
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What will happen to those who add to or subtract from what is commanded by God?
--- Q&A END ---
- They will not be saved Rev. 22:18-19

Who should be baptized
--- Q&A START ---
Q. Who should be baptized, according to the Bible?
A. Those who are taught and who believe .. Mk. 16: 15-16 Note: A person must first be taught and then made sure that he believes what he has been taught before he can be baptized. Therefore, before you can be admitted into the Church of Christ, you must first be taught the fundamental teachings of the Church and undergo screening to make sure that youtruly understand and believe. Even if you have been taught the Gospel but you do not understand and believe, you cannot be baptized.
--- Q&A END ---


--- Q&A START ---
Q. What else is expected from the candidate for baptism?
A. Repentance of his Acts 2:38
--- Q&A END ---

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is sin?
A. Transgression of the law ......... .......... I Jn. 3:4 KJV
--- Q&A END ---

"Whosoever committeth sin transgresseth also the law: for sin is the transgression of the law." '
(Bible. The Holy Bible, containing the Old and New Testaments, translated out of the original tongues and with the former translations diligently compared and revised, set forth in 1611 and commonly known as the King James Version, New York: American Bible Society, 1970.)

U11 l)c,c1>fi;Jr11 111
Conch.tsi(m: Thus, in the Church or Christ. infants are not baptized because they cannot believe yet and they have not transgressed any law. Therefore, they have nothing to repent of.

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is done to the infants in the Church of Christ?
A. They are prayed for or offered ............ Mt. 19: 13-14 RSV
--- Q&A END ---

"Then children were brought to him that he might lay his hands on them and pray. The disciples rebuked the people; but Jesus said, 'Let the children come to me, and do not hinder them; for to such belongs the kingdom of heaven."

[Bible. The Oxford Annotated Bible. The Holy Bible. Revised Standard Version. ed. by Herbert G. Mayand Bruce M. Metzger. New York: Oxford University Press,
(c) 1962.]

Instruction: Do not allow your children to be baptized or confirmed in other religions. If you are already baptized, offer them so that they may be prayed for if they are not yet ready to listen to teachings.

Obligation
--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is the obligation of those who will receive baptism?
A. They should be dead to sin Rom. 6:11 NW
--- Q&A END ---

"In the same way; count yourselves dead to sin but alive to God in Christ Jesus."

[Bible. The Holy Bible. New International Version containing I he Old Te,;tamcnt and the New Testament, New York: International Bible Society, (c) 1978.J

--- Q&A START ---
Q. Why should the candidates for baptism be dead I.a sin?
A. So that the old self may be
crucified with Christ ......................... Rom. 6:6 NIV
--- Q&A END ---

"For we know that our old self was crucified with him so Lhat the body of sin might be done away with, that we should no longer l:ie slaves to sin."

[Bible. 'The Holy Bible. New International Version containing I.he Old Tt:,;t:amcnl. and the New Testament, New York: International Bible Society,(c) 1978.J

--- Q&A START ---
Q. What is this old self that should die and share in the death of Christ on the cross?
A. The corrupt way of life Eph. 4:22 NW
--- Q&A END ---

,,,
